title: Pre-pipeline value probe is 78% of a 5-card page's wall: p50 104.7s / p90 347.9s of :5433 probe time per run
evidence: obs_db_queries Jul12-14: db=target_version1 probe UNIONs = 3,523 queries totaling 46,463s (35.9M ms stage-NULL + 10.5M ms in asset_resolution); per-run burden p50 104,666ms / p90 347,927ms / max 903,113ms (n=293 run traces). Timeline proof t_4302e600f908455eb7f7d5e00b8b3a3d: e2e 236.3s = 183.7s dead gap (9 stage-NULL UNION chunks totaling 183,634ms, sequential) + 52.5s actual pipeline. Call chain: host/server.py:398 natural_compare_ids -> layer1b/resolve/asset_candidates.py asset_candidates() -> data/value_probe.py:55 value_counts() over full ~370-table registry, chunk=40, ~20.4s/chunk.
scenario: cold 5-8-card panel-overview page and any run >120s after the previous one
current_cost: p50 104.7s per run; 183.7s on the sampled 5-card page (78% of 236s e2e); 48% of 294 runs pay >10s (gap p50 1.5s / p90 186.9s)
proposal: MEASUREMENT (ground truth for other lenses): the probe SQL 'ORDER BY timestamp_utc::timestamptz DESC LIMIT 1' seq-scans (EXPLAIN ANALYZE: 641.4ms, 43,430 rows) while the existing index idx_*_tsimm ON (neuract.ts_imm(timestamp_utc::text) DESC) serves the identical row in 0.104ms (6,164x) — ts_imm prosrc = 'SELECT t::timestamptz'. A one-line ORDER BY rewrite at value_probe.py:73 makes the full-registry probe ~0.2s instead of ~184s.
est_saving: -105s p50 / -348s p90 on any run paying the probe; sampled 5-card page 236s -> ~52s (370 tables x 0.6s -> 370 x 0.1ms + 9 RTT x 16ms)

title: l2_emit is ~100% decode-bound: 1,291 completion tok at 73 tok/s = 17.7s of the 18.3s p50 card emit
evidence: obs_llm_calls n=1,289 l2_emit: lat p50 18,304ms / p95 40,134ms; prompt tok p50 21,734 / p95 27,080; completion tok p50 1,291 / p95 2,762; effective decode 73 tok/s p50 (45 p10, 95 p90) under emit_concurrency=4. vLLM /metrics since restart (n=1,237 reqs): decode 8,057.6s = 89.4% of 9,011.7s request wall; prefill 597.8s at 19,226 tok/s effective; queue 0.238s avg; 0 preemptions; 0 retries (100% attempt=0).
scenario: every card on every page; 5-card page L2 wall 36.6s = ceil(5/4) waves x ~19s
current_cost: 1,291 tok / 73 tok/s = 17.7s of 18.3s p50 per card; per-run decode budget p50 5,928 completion tokens
proposal: MEASUREMENT: completion length and decode throughput are the levers, NOT prompt size (prefill = 21.7K/19,226 = 1.13s/card). Halving emitted JSON tokens is worth ~8.8s/card; prefix caching is worth at most 0.44s/card (8.4K sys tok / 19,226 tok/s). Judge other lenses' claims against this split.
est_saving: reference: -8.8s/card per 50% completion-token cut => -18s wall on a 5-card page (2 waves)

title: Probe cache TTL (120s) is shorter than the probe itself (~184s) — cache can never amortize runs spaced >2min
evidence: Gap distribution over 294 runs (knowledge_gate end -> PROMPT marker): bimodal p50 1,505ms (cache hit) vs p90 186,922ms (miss); 140/294 = 48% pay >10s. TTLCache knob cache.resolution_ttl_s=120 (value_probe.py:19-23); measured probe duration ~184s > TTL, and cache key = frozenset(tables) so any differing subset re-probes.
scenario: repeat prompts and any interactive session with >2min think-time between prompts
current_cost: 48% of runs pay the full ~180s probe again
proposal: MEASUREMENT: with M1's index fix the TTL question mostly dissolves (probe ~0.2s); until then, every TTL increase directly converts ~180s misses into ~1.5s hits for runs within the window.
est_saving: -180s for each converted miss; at current traffic ~48% of runs affected

title: vLLM prefix caching is OFF (enable_prefix_caching=False, hybrid-mamba model) — and the prompt has a cache-busting timestamp at char 1,083 anyway
evidence: vllm:cache_config_info shows enable_prefix_caching="False", prefix_cache_queries_total=0 (cache never consulted — explains the 0.0% journal line). Qwen3.6-35B-A3B is hybrid-mamba (mamba_block_size=65536); vLLM disables APC by default for hybrid models; the systemd unit passes no --enable-prefix-caching. l2_emit system prompt IS stable (2 distinct hashes across 1,289 calls, >=32,768 chars each) but user prompts diverge at char 1,083/28,500: a live freshness anchor 'last=2026-07-14T01:53:54...' — only 4% common prefix between emits of the SAME card.
scenario: every l2_emit call
current_cost: prefill 1.13s/card (21.7K tok / 19,226 tok/s), fully re-paid every call
proposal: MEASUREMENT bounds for other lenses: enabling APC (if supported for this hybrid model) + keeping the volatile timestamp OUT of the shared prefix caps the win at ~0.9-1.0s/card (sys 8.4K tok + ~5K shared card text) / 19,226 tok/s); it does NOT touch the 17.7s decode.
est_saving: <=1s/card => <=2s wall on a 5-card page (2 waves); small vs M1/M2

evidence: obs_db_queries: 71,258 neuract queries under executor.card / 1,909 card executions = 37.3/card, p50 58ms, total 5,368s. Live RTT (10x SELECT 1, psycopg2): :5433 min 8.9 / median 15.6 / max 17.0ms; fresh connect 27.7ms; :5432 local 0.01ms. executor.card span p50 1,038ms / p90 6,841ms / max 52,683ms; card fan-out is fully concurrent (8/8 simultaneous starts), so run wall = slowest card (executor run-span p50 1.1s / p90 7.7s).

current_cost: 37 x 15.6ms = 0.58s/card minimum network floor; p90 cards 6.8s
proposal: MEASUREMENT: query-count-per-card and RTT are the two multipliers; batching k queries into one round trip saves (k-1) x 15.6ms each.

title: natural_compare_ids runs serially BEFORE the pipeline and can burn 3+ minutes of blank spinner
scenario: fresh compare-shaped single-asset prompt ('compare voltage and current for pcc 1 and 2')
evidence: Trace t_2aed84a50d9b43a8b8ca1434dfbde5e3: knowledge_gate ended at +0.3s, page_selection/asset_resolution started at +184.6s — the gap is 8 SERIAL has_data batch probes on :5433 (14.4+19.7+23.8+26.6+26.8+27.6+19.3+26.1 = 184.3s) fired by natural_compare_ids' asset_candidates(). Across 302 obs traces the knowledge_gate->pipeline-start gap is p50 1.8s / p90 188.3s / p99 523s / max 538s. Total e2e for that run: 296s, of which 184s was this pre-flight.
current_cost: p50 +1.8s, p90 +188s, p99 +523s added to time-to-anything on every fresh prompt (no asset_id)
proposal: Run natural_compare_ids CONCURRENTLY with an optimistically-started single-asset 1a||1b (promote to build_response_multi only if it returns ids — 1b's own resolution p50 10-22s finishes after the healthy 1.8s check, so the join is nearly free); make it share 1b's candidate/has_data probe instead of a second asset_candidates() call; and stream a progress event meanwhile. Primary root-cause fix (cache/parallelize has_data probes) belongs to the 1b lens — this is the delivery-side de-serialization.
est_saving: -186s p90 / -1.8s p50 time-to-first-byte on fresh prompts (188.3s gap minus ~2s overlapped check)
refined_saving: Time-to-cards from the proposed de-serialization alone: ~-2 to -3s p50, ~0 to -5s p90 (NOT -186s p90 -- the shared probe stays on 1b's critical path; pinned runs prove 1b pays the identical ~209s cold probe when the pre-flight is absent). Plus ~-200 to -420s in the rare (4/318, ~1.3%) outage double-probe runs if the probe is truly shared as one in-flight call. The claimed -186s p90 is real but belongs ~100% to the 1b probe root-cause fix (chunk parallelization: 8 x ~23s serial -> ~27s wall; TTL/background refresh -> ~0 amortized); after that fix lands, this finding's residual value is ~2s p50 + perceived-latency streaming. Recommend re-scoping this finding to: (a) share one in-flight probe (dedupe/coalesce concurrent cold probes), (b) progress streaming for perceived latency -- and let the 1b lens carry the -186s headline.
risk_notes: No AI-first / per-leaf-honesty / CMD_V2-read-only violations (host orchestration only). Real risks of the proposal as written: (1) the optimistically-started single-asset lane burns wasted 1b/L2 LLM emits whenever promotion to multi fires -- vLLM contention against layer2.emit_concurrency=4 (known contention gotcha); (2) promotion race: the optimistic lane must be discarded/cancelled cleanly or the byte-identical single-asset contract is at risk (it holds only if the optimistic lane IS build_response unchanged); (3) natural_compare_ids' fail-open boundary (multi_asset.py:31-34, outage -> [] -> single path degrade gate) must survive the concurrent join -- an exception in the concurrent pre-flight must still never 500 the request; (4) 'stream a progress event' is a new FE contract (api.ts) + server model change (one-shot JSON on ThreadingHTTPServer today) -- medium effort understated. Overlap warning: implement the 1b probe fix first; measure again before spending the medium effort here.
verify_notes: Mechanism and cost numbers verified exactly. server.py:399 runs natural_compare_ids serially before the pipeline on every fresh prompt; multi_asset.py:49 -> asset_candidates() -> tables_with_values() (data/value_probe.py:55-90) loops SERIALLY over chunks of 40 tables, each a UNION-ALL last-row jsonb probe on :5433. Cited trace t_2aed84a5: 8 serial db spans of 14.4/19.7/23.8/26.6/26.8/27.6/19.3/26.1s = 184.3s between knowledge_gate end (+0.3s) and first page_selection span (+184.6s); run total 296.1s. Population recomputed over 318 usable trace jsonls (claim said 302 via DB): gap p50 2.5s / p90 197.9s / p99 396.5s / max 533s vs claimed 1.8/188.3/523/538 -- same bimodal story (160 warm <5s, 155 cold >=120s). Gaps are CURRENT: 07-13 p50 177.6s, 07-14 p50 183.5s; live cache.resolution_ttl_s=120 drives the ~50% cold rate. HOWEVER the saving arithmetic is wrong (see refined_saving): the probe is TTL-cache-deduped today (there is NO 'second asset_candidates() call' -- pre-flight warms, 1b hits warm: fresh-run asset_resolution p50 8.6s), and pinned runs that skip the pre-flight pay the SAME cold probe inside asset_resolution (p50 208.8s / p90 222.6s, n=52) -- proof the 184s is the cold-probe cost, not incremental pre-flight cost. De-serialization relocates it onto 1b's critical path; L2/executor are all downstream of 1b, so time-to-cards barely moves. Also /api/run has no streaming today (single JSON from ThreadingHTTPServer), so 'time-to-first-byte' = e2e unless new streaming infra is built. The real -186s belongs to the probe fix (parallelize the 8 independent chunks -> ~27s, and/or TTL>120s + background refresh) which the finding itself assigns to the 1b lens -- near-100% overlap. Genuine residual extras of THIS proposal: single shared in-flight probe kills the outage double-probe pattern (4/318 fresh traces paid gap + cold asset_resolution again, 380-612s in one run, e.g. t_47ca0099 197s+415s, because an outage-raise caches nothing and 1b re-probes); and a progress event fixes blank-spinner PERCEPTION only.

title: Monolithic JSON response — stream shell + per-card NDJSON instead
scenario: healthy cold single-asset dashboard (n=97 traces, avg 4.2 cards)
evidence: Everything waits for the last card: healthy-regime percentiles from obs_stage_events — shell-ready (validation end) p50 9.8s/p90 16.0s, first L2 emit done p50 23.8s/p90 35.4s, full response p50 42.0s/p90 81.6s. FE shows a static RunningCard spinner the whole time (App.tsx:233-246). exec fan-out already yields per-card via as_completed (host/exec_cards.py:254) and enrich is already per-card (host/assemble.py:54) — the seams for per-card emission exist.
current_cost: first paint = full e2e: 42s p50 / 81.6s p90 healthy; 142s p50 multi
proposal: Flag-gated POST /api/run?stream=1 emitting chunked NDJSON on the same ThreadingHTTPServer: {ev:shell,page,layout,card stubs} after asset_gate; {ev:card,<enriched card>} per exec completion; {ev:done,<today's exact envelope>} terminal. FE: cards as Map<card_id,Card>, HonestBlankTile as skeleton, CardGrid mounts incrementally. Non-stream path stays byte-identical (pinned contract untouched).
est_saving: -32s p50 / -65s p90 to first paint (shell at 9.8s vs 42s); -16s p50 to first real card (~26s vs 42s); multi-asset: first group at lane-1 end (~40-60s) vs 142s p50
refined_saving: First paint (shell): -31.7s p50 (9.8 vs 41.5) / -63.9s p90 (17.9 vs 81.8) — CONFIRMED, but only in the healthy no-queue regime (104/228 = ~46% of ok runs). First real card: -5.3s p50 / -19.5s p90 as proposed (exec-completion streaming); the claimed -16s p50 requires an additional L2->exec per-card pipelining restructure not in this proposal. Multi: baseline is p50 48.3s / p90 224.8s (not 142s); median first-group gain is modest as proposed (exec loop is post-all-lanes), large gains only at p90 heavy compares or with lane-interleaved assembly.
risk_notes: (1) OVERLAP: ~54% of ok traces are in a contention regime with a ~180s pre-route LLM queue gap (example trace: knowledge_gate ends 0.2s, page_selection starts 184.0s) — streaming buys ZERO there since even the shell waits behind the queue; an LLM admission/contention fix dominates and this finding's savings apply only after it. Also overlaps any L2-emit-latency fix: l2 last emit p50 35.0s of a 41.5s response — if emit time halves, full e2e drops toward ~22s and the first-paint delta shrinks from -32s to ~-12s. (2) Shell staleness: post-emit reflect re-route (reflect.reroute_on='hard_failure') can swap page_key/layout/cards after the shell streams — needs a shell-replace/reset event; L2 swap_decision and section-overlay gates change render card identity vs 1a stubs — per-card events must replace stubs keyed by card_id. (3) Wrapper restructure: obs middleware run_traced + replay capture + _dump_response assume a single terminal resp dict; the ev:done envelope must still feed them or traces/replay artifacts silently break. (4) exec budget path (ER-8): budget-exceeded stragglers currently honest-blank in the final sweep — the stream must emit those blank-card events too or the FE grid hangs on skeletons.
verify_notes: Mechanism verified line-by-line: server.py _send (198-207) writes one Content-Length JSON body after handle_run fully returns (285-286); App.tsx run() (73-106) awaits the whole response behind a static RunningCard spinner (233-246); exec fan-out yields per-card via as_completed (exec_cards.py:254) and enrich is a per-card function (assemble.py:54) — the seams exist. Stage order confirmed in run/harness.py: 1a||1b -> validate -> asset_gate -> L2 emit fan-out + reflect -> (host) executor -> enrich, so the shell (page/layout/card stubs) genuinely exists at asset_gate time. Numbers REPRODUCED from obs_stage_events (cmd_catalog): filtering ok kind='run' traces with >0 cards and no pre-route queue gap (page_selection starts <5s after trace start) gives n=104 (claim said 97): shell-ready p50 9.8s/p90 17.9s (claim 9.8/16.0), first L2 emit done p50 23.2/p90 35.5 (claim 23.8/35.4), full p50 41.5/p90 81.8 (claim 42.0/81.6) — match. TWO corrections: (a) first executor.card COMPLETION is p50 36.2s/p90 62.3s because the executor only starts after ALL L2 emits + reflect settle (last L2 emit p50 35.0s) — so the claimed '-16s p50 to first real card (~26s)' is NOT achievable with the proposal as written (stream per exec completion); it requires additionally pipelining per-card exec ahead of the reflect loop, a deeper restructure that conflicts with reflect's full-emit inspection, payload_final re-scoring, and swap-driven card-identity changes. As written, first-real-card gain is ~5.3s p50 / ~19.5s p90. (b) Multi claim unverified: no kind='multi' obs traces exist; RESPONSE_MULTI pipeline logs give p50 48.3s/p90 224.8s (n=134 real runs), not 142s p50, and build_response_multi's exec loop runs AFTER all lanes' L2, so first-group streaming at 'lane-1 end' also needs lane-interleaved assembly (further restructure). No hard-constraint violations: flag-gated stream=1 leaves the pinned non-stream path byte-identical; skeleton stubs (HonestBlankTile) preserve per-leaf honesty; CMD_V2 untouched (FE changes are host/web); no per-card hardcode. Effort 'large' is fair: _traced_captured/replay capture/_dump_response/obs middleware all assume fn()->(code,resp) and need a streaming-aware wrapper fed by the terminal ev:done envelope; _send's Content-Length path means hand-rolled chunked transfer encoding on the ThreadingHTTPServer (stdlib-feasible, HTTP/1.1 already set).

title: Repeat prompts recompute 1a + every L2 emit; cache the AI recipe, re-fill only data
scenario: repeat prompt within minutes (same prompt, same asset)
evidence: run_id = make_run_id(prompt) is deterministic yet a re-POST re-runs route/stories/page_selection (~5-10s) and all L2 emits (p50 19.8s/card, layer2 wall p50 31.7s). Recipe reuse is already proven sound: the multi path rebinds ONE L2 recipe across N assets (host/multi_asset.py:130 rebind_consumer), byte-tested; outputs/logs/response_<rid>.json already persists the full recipe.
current_cost: repeat prompt costs full p50 42s (healthy) though only exec (5.7s tail) touches fresh data
proposal: DB-knob-gated TTL cache (10-15min) keyed (prompt, resolved asset, page_key) holding {layer1a, layer2 recipe}; hit => skip 1a+L2, run assemble_cards (exec fan-out) + enrich on fresh neuract reads. AI-first preserved: cached artifacts ARE the AI's decisions for that exact prompt; data always re-read per-leaf-honest.
est_saving: -32s p50 on a repeat prompt (42s -> ~6-10s exec+enrich); panel p90 81.6s -> ~12-15s
refined_saving: Per repeat prompt (same prompt+asset within TTL): p50 42.0s -> ~4-6s = -36s p50 (slightly BETTER than the claimed -32s); p90 residual ~24s (executor-bound). Panel repeats: p50 61.4s -> ~17s (-44s), p90 190.1s -> ~35s (not the claimed 12-15s — panel executor fan-out is itself p90 34.4s and becomes the new bottleneck). Fleet-wide saving = these numbers x repeat fraction (83% in the sweep-heavy mined corpus; real interactive rate unverified).
risk_notes: (1) Cache-poison hazard is the top risk: MUST skip caching on data_unavailable / asset_pending / validation_blocked / emit-gap runs — the v48 member-cache-poison incident (never-cache-empty + TTL) is the exact precedent; a tunnel flap cached for 15min would poison every repeat. (2) Validation/1b verdicts go stale within TTL (asset going dark mid-window serves cards that exec will honestly blank — acceptable, verdicts are telemetry not render gates). (3) Replay engine (replay/capture wraps handle_run) needs a cache-bypass knob or replays stop exercising 1a/L2. (4) Cached notes/loop records replay verbatim (cosmetic). (5) OVERLAP: saving overlaps every cold-path L2 fix (S-emit-concurrency / token-budget / has_data-probe cache) — those shrink the 42s baseline this fix skips; do not sum. Once repeats are ~free the bottleneck moves to the executor, especially panel_aggregate member fan-out (p90 34s) — a separate exec-side lever.
verify_notes: Mechanism verified line-by-line: run/run_id.py:5 make_run_id is a pure sha1(prompt); host/server.py handle_run (l.349) -> build_response (l.61) -> run_pipeline(prompt) with ZERO caching anywhere on the serve path (grepped run/harness.py + layer1a/ — no cache), so a re-POST re-runs knowledge gate + 1a + 1b + stories + validation + every L2 emit. Recipe-reuse precedent is exactly as claimed: host/multi_asset.py ~l.131 rebinds ONE class recipe via host/rebind_consumer.py (envelope-only repoint, AI fields copied verbatim) then assemble_cards refills per asset; response_<rid>.json persists cards carrying exact_metadata+data_instructions (the same recipe /api/frame refills from). Freshness story holds structurally: concrete date window is resolved AFTER run_pipeline (build_response l.79-82 window_from_preset) and all leaf data is read at serve time in host/assemble.py assemble_cards — so a {layer1a, layer2, resolved-asset} cache keeps dates and per-leaf honesty fresh. Numbers re-derived from outputs/latency/mined_samples.json (1,287 runs): healthy-run e2e p50 = 42.0s EXACTLY as claimed; layer2 wall p50 31.7s, per-card 19.8s, route/resolve wall p50 8.3s all match. Cache-hit residual = gate ~0.7s + per-run executor (p50 3.3s, p90 23.6s, p95 34.4s) + render ~40ms. ONE sub-claim fails: 'panel p90 81.6s -> 12-15s' — panel-prompt healthy e2e is actually p50 61.4s / p90 190.1s (81.6s not reproducible from profiler data), and panel per-run executor ALONE is p50 15.9s / p90 34.4s (panel_aggregate member fan-out), so a repeat panel prompt lands ~17-35s, not 12-15s. Repeat scenario is real in the observed corpus: 83% of mined executions were repeats of an already-seen prompt (213 unique / 1,287 runs) — but that corpus is validation-sweep-dominated; interactive repeat fraction unknown, so fleet-wide p50 gain depends on it. No hard-constraint violations: cached artifacts ARE the AI's decisions (AI-first ok), data re-read per leaf every serve (honesty ok), CMD_V2 untouched, miss path byte-identical, knob-gated default-off keeps suite green.

title: Multi-asset compare: class lanes AND per-asset exec assembles run sequentially, plus a trailing compare_mode LLM call
scenario: 3-feeder compare (RESPONSE_MULTI p50 142s / p90 683s live)
evidence: run_pipeline_multi loops `for cls, members in by_class.items()` serially (each lane = full 1b+validate+L2); build_response_multi then loops `for asset in group['assets']` calling assemble_cards serially (each a 5.7s-p50 exec fan-out, panel fills longer); compare_mode (one LLM classification, ~0.5s) runs only AFTER all fills though it needs just the prompt.
current_cost: (N_assets-1) x exec-fan-out serial (~5.7-10s each) + (N_classes-1) x full lane serial (~40s each) + 0.5s compare_mode tail
proposal: (a) ThreadPool the per-asset assemble_cards calls (executor pool pattern already thread-safe); (b) run class lanes concurrently gated at 2 (respect layer2.emit_concurrency=4 as the shared cap); (c) fire compare_mode concurrently with the fills; (d) with S1, stream group 1 while group 2 fills.
est_saving: -11s on a 3-same-class compare (3x5.7s -> 6s); -25-30s p50 on a 2-class compare (2x~40s lanes -> ~50s overlapped); -0.5s compare_mode; largest wins on the 410-441s worst runs
refined_saving: (a) parallel per-asset fills: -4 to -8s p50 / ~-11 to -16s avg on a 3-same-class compare, up to -40s+ on panel-heavy p95 fills; applies to ~68% of live multi runs — this is the real win. (b) concurrent class lanes: -10 to -20s realistic (NOT -25-30s) because both lanes' L2 emits share vLLM :8200 capacity — under a strictly-shared emit_concurrency=4 cap the L2 overlap saving approaches zero (throughput-bound); and it applies to only ~2.5% of live multi runs. (c) concurrent compare_mode: -0.3 to -1.5s, unverified. Combined on the 410-442s worst runs: new wall = slowest lane + its fills, not the sum — expect ~-60 to -120s there, but those runs are dominated by one pathological lane (1b/L2 stall) that parallelism does not fix.
risk_notes: Overlaps: (d) explicitly depends on S1 streaming — do not double-count; any accepted L2-emit speedup finding (layer2 dominates at 19.8s p50/card) proportionally shrinks (b)'s absolute saving; an executor/fill speedup finding would shrink (a). Implementation risks: (1) vLLM contention — memory records emit_concurrency=4 exists because higher fan-out caused cert 'llm timeout' failures; concurrent lanes MUST share one semaphore across lanes or they re-introduce that regression; (2) obs/ai_log: lane threads must propagate contextvars (use run/parallel's copy-context pattern) and the ai_log process-global run-id fallback races across plain threads — same defect family as the console-validation fake-stage attribution bug; (3) card ordering must stay deterministic on concat (collect futures in submit order) or FE grouping/overlay merge_all and ordering-sensitive tests break; (4) parallel fills multiply concurrent neuract queries (3 assets x 8 workers = 24) over the :5433 tunnel — pool overflow is allowed but tunnel flap blast radius grows (member-cache-poison history).
verify_notes: Mechanism 100% verified against today's code: (1) run/harness.py:409-416 loops class lanes strictly serially — each lane a full run_pipeline (pinned 1b + validate + L2 emit; lanes 2+ skip 1a via the shared_1a injection); (2) host/multi_asset.py:126-139 loops per-asset assemble_cards serially, each an executor ThreadPool fan-out (exec_cards.py:250, <=8 workers per call); (3) compare_mode(prompt) at multi_asset.py:145-146 runs only after all fills yet takes only the prompt — trivially concurrent-firable, fail-open. Thread-safety premises hold: obs is contextvars-based with an existing copy-context fan-out helper (run/parallel.py), DB layer is pooled psycopg2, asset pinning is parameter-passed (no env-var clobber hazard). Cost is real: 405 live RESPONSE_MULTI records show p90 74.5s / p95 204s / max 441.7s, and the top-4 worst (410-442s, assets 2-3) match the claim's worst band. Per-lane ~40s holds (L2 all-cards median 31.7-32.6s + pinned 1b + validate). Per-fill 5.7s p50 sits between mined median 2.4s and live median 4.0s (avg 6.2-9.4s, panel p95 18-30s) — right magnitude. CORRECTIONS: the claimed 'RESPONSE_MULTI p50 142s / p90 683s' does NOT reproduce (real p50 ~0s from degenerate records; nothing above 441.7s exists); only 10/405 (2.5%) of live multi runs have 2+ classes, so lane-parallelization (b) is tail-only while per-asset fill parallelization (a) covers 277/405 runs; compare_mode cost is unverified (0 live samples, feature 1 day old). No hard-constraint violation: changes are multi-path-only (N==1 short-circuits before this file), per-leaf honesty and AI-first untouched. Caveat for (b): lanes 2+ depend on lane 1's layer1a, so concurrency needs a small restructure (route 1a once upfront ~2s, then fan lanes) — not a bare ThreadPool wrap.

title: Page shell (page_key/layout/card list) is final at ~10s but renders at ~42s — ship a two-phase response even without full streaming
scenario: healthy cold single-asset dashboard
evidence: Shell-ready (post asset_gate/validation) measured p50 9.8s / p90 16.0s vs response p50 42.0s / p90 81.6s (97 healthy traces). Caveat measured: granularity_reconcile swapped the page at +11.7s in r_48c83f28a2, so the shell must be emitted AFTER asset_gate (post reconcile+preflight); reflect re-routes are rare under reroute_on=hard_failure and can be handled with a shell_replace event.
current_cost: 32s p50 / 65s p90 of blank 'Working…' spinner while the grid geometry is already decided
proposal: Subset of S1 shippable alone: chunked two-event response (shell event at asset_gate, final monolith at end); FE renders CardGrid with HonestBlankTile skeletons per known card_id, then swaps in the full result.
est_saving: -32s p50 / -65s p90 time-to-first-meaningful-paint on every dashboard prompt
refined_saving: -31s p50 / -61 to -65s p90 time-to-first-meaningful-paint (measured per-trace delta: 30.7s p50, 65.0s p90, n=102 healthy traces), on healthy UNCONTENDED single-asset dashboard runs only. Zero e2e/compute saving — perceived latency only. Scope correction: 55% of current ok-traces (126/228) stall ~190s BEFORE the pipeline even starts (gap between knowledge_gate and PROMPT under concurrent/sweep load — natural-compare preflight + queueing); those runs still gain the same ~30s absolute delta but first paint stays ~3min, so 'every dashboard prompt' overstates — say 'every uncontended dashboard prompt'. Overlap: fully subsumed by S1 (finding admits subset — do NOT double-count). The delta shrinks 1:1 with any L2-emit/executor speedup (delta IS layer2+executor wall: profiler l2 wall p50 31.7s); conversely a 1b has_data-cache fix would pull shell-ready from ~10s to ~3-5s, increasing this fix's perceived value.
risk_notes: Risk is low-medium, not low: (1) obs middleware (_traced_captured/run_traced), replay capture (replay/capture.captured), and _dump_response all wrap a fn returning the COMPLETE resp dict — a streamed response needs a new seam in each, or events must be emitted from inside run_pipeline via a callback/queue while the wrappers keep seeing the final monolith. (2) sweep/validation/admin/replay consumers parse a single JSON body from /api/run — the two-event protocol MUST be a separate opt-in endpoint (e.g. NDJSON /api/run/stream) with the final event byte-identical, or the 30k-prompt validation harness and replay engine break. (3) Shell must carry asset_pending/validation_blocked/data_unavailable so the FE opens the picker/notice instead of a skeleton grid — all available at asset_gate. (4) Multi-asset lane (build_response_multi) needs its own shell semantics — out of the measured scope. (5) Skeleton tiles are transient loading UI, not render verdicts — no per-leaf-honesty violation, but keep them generic (L2 swaps can change a slot's component before final).
verify_notes: Mechanism verified in code: build_response (host/server.py:61-144) blocks on run_pipeline and Handler._send writes one Content-Length JSON — no intermediate emission exists today. In run/harness.py the shell inputs (page_key/page_title/layout/groups/cards/asset/validation/pending flags/window) are ALL final at the asset_gate stage (line 347), which sits AFTER granularity_reconcile (line 296) and preflight_reroute (line 315) and BEFORE the Layer-2 _reflect_loop (line 363) — so the proposed emission point is correct. The final FE card list (host/assemble.py:53-61) iterates the SAME l1a.cards list the shell would carry, so skeleton grid geometry == final geometry in the healthy case (L2 swap_decision only changes per-slot component/payload; reflect re-routes under reroute_on='hard_failure' were 0 in observed data — every legacy.reflect event was honest_terminal/skipped — so shell_replace is a rare escape hatch, as claimed). The r_48c83f28a2 reconcile page-swap evidence is real in obs_stage_events. FE spinner cost is real (host/web/src/App.tsx:238-241 'Working' / 'Building your real-time view…'). Numbers re-derived from obs_traces/obs_stage_events: healthy subset (kind=run, status=ok, gate<60s, n=102 vs claimed 97): shell-ready p50 9.6s / p90 15.5s vs response p50 41.1s / p90 76.8s, per-trace delta p50 30.7s / p90 65.0s — matches the claim within ~1s. No hard-constraint violation IF shipped as an opt-in second protocol (new endpoint or content-negotiated) with the final event byte-identical to today's /api/run body.

title: Executor fills start only after the LAST L2 emit — pipeline them per-emit
scenario: cold single-asset dashboard; prerequisite for S1's first-card time
evidence: Trace A: L2 emits finished at +14.2/+21.8/+27.5/+36.6/+93.8s after L2 start, yet ALL executor.card spans start at the last emit (+93.8s); exec tail p50 5.7s (healthy regime). Emit spread first->last p50 11s / p90 34.6s means early cards idle 11-90s before their 1-6s fill.
current_cost: exec tail 5.7s p50 fully serial after L2; early cards' data is 11-35s staler than it could be
proposal: Fill each card as its emit completes (queue/callback from run_2_all instead of dict-join). swap_settle is a post-pass over all emits: either fill non-swapped emits eagerly and re-fill the rare reverted swap, or settle-then-fill per card optimistically. Keeps per-leaf degradation untouched.
est_saving: -3-5s p50 e2e (exec tail overlapped except last card's own ~1-3s fill); with S1, first card lands at first-emit+~2s (=~26s) instead of last-emit+exec (=42s)
refined_saving: Standalone: -1.7s p50 / -3.5s p75 / -5.8s p90 e2e (optimistic upper bound; each fill starts at its own emit end). As S1 prerequisite: first-card perceived latency drops ~15.5s p50 / ~50s p90 (first card at first-emit+own-fill = p50 15.7s after L2 start, vs all-cards p50 33.8s). Do not book the claimed -3-5s p50; book ~-2s p50 e2e standalone plus the S1-contingent perceived gain.
risk_notes: OVERLAPS: (1) any executor speedup (psycopg2 pool already live; further exec-tail cuts shrink S13's absolute saving proportionally — saving is bounded by exec tail 5.35s p50); (2) any fix compressing the L2 emit spread (prompt shrinking, higher emit_concurrency) reduces the overlap window S13 exploits; (3) do not double-count with S1 — S13's 1.7s e2e and S1's 15.5s perceived first-card are different metrics on the same mechanism. IMPLEMENTATION RISKS: crosses the harness/host boundary (run_pipeline is pure, executor lives at host) — needs a queue/callback seam through run_2_all; multi-asset build_response_multi reuses ONE per-class L2 dict across N lanes, so per-emit fills must fan per lane; source-locked wiring test (test_build_response_wires_attach) and the byte-compatible assemble_cards contract constrain the refactor; reflect-loop reroute discards loop-1 emits so eager fills there are wasted compute (rare, ~4% of traces have reflect events); eager fills make early cards' data older at delivery by the emit spread (11-35s) — immaterial for minute-level data but the finding claimed the opposite direction.
verify_notes: MECHANISM VERIFIED in code and in 241 real traces. Code: /home/rohith/desktop/BFI/backend/layer2/pipeline_v48/run/layer2_all.py:70 `run_parallel(tasks, max_workers=cap)` is a full dict-join — run_2_all returns only after ALL emits + the swap_settle post-pass; run/harness.py:118 receives the completed dict; the executor fan-out (host/assemble.py:40 -> host/exec_cards._run_cards) runs only after run_pipeline returns. Measured from cmd_catalog obs_stage_events (241 single-asset traces with >=2 emits): first executor.card starts a median 17ms / p90 23ms after the LAST layer2_card_ai.card span ends; 236/241 traces strictly after (the 5 exceptions are overlapping-lane traces). Cited Trace A exists byte-exactly: t_2aed84a50d9b43a8b8ca1434dfbde5e3, emits at +14.2/+21.8/+27.5/+36.6/+93.8s, ALL 5 executor.card spans start at +93.8s, exec tail 93.8->99.5s = 5.7s. Population numbers: emit spread first->last p50 11.2s (claim: 11s, exact) / p90 42.4s (claim said 34.6s — theirs is a slightly filtered subset, direction unchanged); exec tail p50 5.35s (claim 5.7s, ok). SAVING ARITHMETIC OVERSTATED ~2x: card_id-matched optimistic pipelining bound (each fill starts exactly at its own emit end) gives e2e saving p50 1.68s / p75 3.5s / p90 5.8s / mean 2.6s / max 31.8s — NOT -3-5s p50 — because the last-emitting card still needs its own fill and slow fills correlate with late emits. The S1-contingent first-card claim is real and actually conservative: first card could be ready p50 15.7s after L2 start vs all-cards-done p50 33.8s -> perceived gain p50 ~15.5s / p90 ~50s, but ONLY with per-card streaming (S1); standalone (single JSON response) the user sees only the ~1.7s p50. CONSTRAINTS: no violation — orchestration-only, per-leaf fill logic untouched, AI-first intact, CMD_V2 untouched. Feasibility strengthened by data: 0 swap_revert events in all recorded traces (the 're-fill reverted swap' branch would essentially never fire); reflect events in only ~11 of ~300 traces (wasted eager fills on a reroute are rare, compute-only). One evidence error worth noting: 'early cards data is 11-35s staler than it could be' is BACKWARDS — filling earlier makes data staler at delivery, not fresher; immaterial for minute-granularity time-series but it is a (small) cost of the proposal, not of the status quo.

title: Picker re-POST recomputes 1a (and 1b candidates) from zero for the identical prompt
scenario: ambiguous prompt -> picker -> pinned re-POST
evidence: Measured 4 round trips in pipeline logs: leg1 (pending, no L2) 3.9-6.2s; leg2 (pinned) re-spends 1.4-6.1s on the 1a/1b re-run (28% of a 22.1s pinned run) — and 209.6s in the contended case (pipeline_r_fc900dffea). run_pipeline already accepts layer1a= injection (the multi path uses it, harness.py:242) so the reuse seam exists and is test-covered.
current_cost: +1.4-6.1s p50 (up to +210s contended) on every picker interaction; ~28% of leg2
proposal: Host-side 60-120s TTL cache {prompt -> layer1a + validation + candidate_list} written on the asset_pending return; a re-POST of the SAME prompt with asset_id(s) injects the cached 1a via the existing layer1a= parameter. AI-first: the cached 1a IS the AI's routing for that prompt.
est_saving: -1.4-6.1s p50 per picker flow (-28% of a 22s pinned leg); -200s tail under contention
refined_saving: ~0s p50, ~1.1s p90, <=2.5s max per picker interaction (only the 12% of legs where the 1a re-run outlasts the parallel pinned-1b basket call). Not -1.4-6.1s p50 and not -200s tail. Conditional caveat: IF a separate fix makes the pinned-1b basket call fast (<~2s), a 1a cache would then be worth up to ~1.9s p50 per picker flow -- i.e. this idea only pays AFTER the basket-latency finding is fixed, and its saving fully overlaps/depends on that fix.
risk_notes: The proposal as written is also a correctness hazard: injecting cached 1a via the existing layer1a= seam sets _shared_template=True, which LOCKS the page -- suppressing reconcile_granularity (run/harness.py:295) and the pre-L2 preflight reroute (harness.py:314) and reflect re-routes. The picker leg2 is the FIRST run where a resolved asset exists, so granularity reconcile (single meter pinned onto a panel-aggregate shell, routing #07) must run there; reusing the multi-compare locked-page seam would silently disable it. A picker-reuse variant would need a new inject-but-unlocked flag (new seam, not the test-covered one the finding cites). Also caching leg1 'validation' is invalid: leg1 validated with no asset (verdict asset_pending); leg2 validation is asset-dependent and must re-run.
verify_notes: Mechanism is real but the saving is illusory due to parallelism. Confirmed: handle_run pinned re-POST (host/server.py:407 -> build_response server.py:63) calls run_pipeline without layer1a=, so run/harness.py:242 re-runs 1a from zero; 164 real picker round trips found in outputs/logs (pinned-leg 1a/1b block p50 6.6s, the finding's exact 6.1s/22.1s/27.6% example exists in pipeline_r_f37fab9b3c). HOWEVER 1a and 1b run in PARALLEL (run_parallel, harness.py:241-244) and the pinned 1b leg is the long pole: build_basket makes its own LLM call (layer1b/basket/column_basket.py call_qwen) which leg1 NEVER made (ambiguous leg1 -> asset=None -> basket skipped), so leg2's basket call is first-time work, not redundant recompute. Measured from obs_stage_events (124 pinned legs): pinned asset_resolution p50 8.4s vs 1a total (page_selection 0.47s + story_selection 1.54s) p50 1.85s; saving from removing 1a = max(1a,1b)-1b = 0.0s p50 / 1.07s p90 / 2.5s max; 1a outlasts pinned-1b in only 12% of legs. The -200s contended tail claim is misattributed: in r_fc900dffea leg2 the spans show page_selection=0.31s + story_selection=1.01s while pinned asset_resolution=209.6s -- the entire contended block was 1b's basket LLM call/probes; 1a completed in 1.3s in the same contended window, so caching 1a saves ~nothing in the tail. The real leg2 pre-L2 lever is the pinned-1b path (basket LLM latency/timeout+transient-retry, candidate has_data probes over :5433), which belongs to a different finding.

title: /api/frame date-change: one POST per card (browser 6-connection cap) and zero pending/error UI
scenario: date change on an 8-card panel-overview page
evidence: obs_traces kind=frame n=262: p50 1.68s / p90 7.9s / p99 16.1s per card. DateSync makes EVERY is_history card fire its own POST; HTTP/1.1 allows 6 concurrent per origin, so cards 7-8 queue ~8s p90 behind the first wave. Old data stays visible with no spinner and errors are swallowed (`.catch(() => {})`) — stale vs fresh is indistinguishable.
current_cost: 8-card date change worst lane ~= 2x p90 ~= 16s, perceived as 'nothing happening'
proposal: (a) per-card in-flight overlay + error badge in CmdCard (~20 FE lines, immediate honesty win); (b) batch POST /api/frames with N cards — server fans out on the existing exec pool, breaking the connection cap and producing 1 trace instead of N.
est_saving: -8s p90 on an 8-card date change (queue eliminated: max single ~= 8s) + immediate perceived feedback at 0s
refined_saving: Split the two sub-proposals. (a) Per-card in-flight overlay + error badge: unconditional, page-size-independent — perceived feedback at 0s and un-swallows errors; this is the confirmed immediate win. (b) Batch /api/frames: -4 to -7s wall on pages with >6 is_history cards (claimed -8s is the zero-contention ceiling: 10-card burst 24.7s -> ~16.7s if all start at t=0, but 6->8-10 concurrent fills adds contention inflation); ~0s on pages with <=6 history cards (no browser queue exists there — the observed 6-card bursts at 15.7s wall are pure server contention, untouched by batching). Secondary benefit of (b): 1 obs trace instead of N.
risk_notes: Overlap warning: the dominant latent lever is per-frame DB fan-in — 52-248 sequential queries per frame over the single :5433 SSH tunnel, inflating per-card latency ~4-8x under concurrency (uncontended ~1-2s vs contended 6-16s). Any sibling finding that reduces per-frame query count, batches queries, or fixes tunnel head-of-line blocking will shrink both the per-card p90 AND the queue this fix eliminates — double-counting risk if both savings are summed. Implementation risks are low: batch endpoint must remain a pure fan-out over the existing handle_frame path (window-gate logic per handling_class, RC2b consumer.range override, member_scope) to keep payloads byte-identical; the overlay must be a loading state, never a render gate (per-leaf degradation rule). Also note frame p90 will look better than reality in blended obs stats because replay-tool traffic (sequential, warm) dilutes browser-burst numbers — evaluate post-fix impact on burst wall-time, not blended p90.
verify_notes: Mechanism verified in code and numbers reproduced exactly from obs_traces. (1) CmdCard.tsx:46-51 fires one POST per is_history card on every shared-window change; fetchCardFrame (host/web/src/api.ts:150-165) throws on failure but CmdCard swallows it with `.catch(() => {})` and the only state is a silent payloadOverride swap — zero pending/error UI, exactly as claimed. (2) server.py:279-281 handles one card per /api/frame POST; the server is ThreadingHTTPServer (line 417), so the serializer is client-side: the browser 6-per-origin HTTP/1.1 cap via the Vite :5188 proxy. (3) obs_traces kind='frame': n=262, p50 1681 / p90 7916 / p99 16083 ms — matches the finding to the millisecond. (4) Direct queue signature found in a real 10-card browser burst (2026-07-13 04:21): card 9 starts at +7.81s exactly when card 7 completes (+7.80s), card 10 at +8.05s exactly when card 1 completes (+8.04s); wall 24.7s. Caveats that REFINE (not refute): latency_ms is server-side, so browser queue time is additive on top of the cited percentiles; and the 262-trace population is polluted by sequential replay-tool traffic (~104 traces on 07-14 in groups-of-4 identical n_db_queries at ~1-1.4s) — real browser bursts (6 concurrent) run 5.9-16.7s/card, i.e. worse than the blended p90. Material discovery: uncontended frames cost ~0.7-2s; the 6-16s figures are concurrency-inflated (52-248 sequential DB queries per frame over the :5433 tunnel; ~4-8x inflation at 6-way concurrency), so server/tunnel contention — not the browser cap — is the larger half of the wall time. No constraint violations: overlay/error badge is host-FE loading/telemetry UI (not CMD_V2, not a render gate — per-leaf honesty untouched); batch /api/frames reuses fill_one_card per card (byte-identical payloads) on the existing exec pool (exec_cards.py:250, max_workers=8); additive endpoint, suite-green plausible.

title: Production FE served by `npx vite` dev mode — 250 module requests / 5.3s load; a built bundle exists unserved
scenario: first browser load / hard refresh of :5188
evidence: Measured with a real browser on localhost (warm Vite cache): 250 module requests, 3.8MB transferred, load event 5.33s (FCP 468ms but the eager card-registry graph completes at 5.3s). 211 static @cmd-v2 imports + eager import.meta.glob barrels load the entire CMD_V2 graph at startup. dist/ build (2.6MB, AdminApp code-split, Jul 12) exists but is not what the service serves. LAN adds RTT x 250 requests.
current_cost: 5.3s localhost / est 7-10s LAN per first load or hard refresh; dev transform CPU competes with the pipeline on the same box
proposal: Serve the build: `vite build && vite preview --port 5188` (or static dist/ behind a tiny server with the same /api,/copilot,/admin proxies). Keep dev mode for development. Optionally lazy-load the admin/inspector chunks (already split in build).
est_saving: -3-8s per first load / hard refresh; frees dev-server CPU during runs
refined_saving: -5.5s per first load / hard refresh on localhost (measured: 5,609ms dev vs 113ms built — larger than the claimed 5.3s baseline because load-event, not FCP, is the honest end mark); est -6 to -9s on LAN (>=250 requests x RTT vs 13 requests, 3.8MB vs 2.5MB). Saving applies only to first-load/hard-refresh, not per prompt; zero overlap with pipeline e2e latency fixes (l2_emit etc. are orthogonal). Drop the 'frees pipeline CPU' component from the savings ledger (~negligible). After the fix the next FE-bootstrap bottleneck is the 2.5MB monolithic index chunk (~0.1-1s scale) — not worth chasing yet.
risk_notes: Low risk but three operational caveats: (1) dist/ is stale (built Jul 12 08:24; live bus-section-overlay/PCC bugfix work landed after) — the switch REQUIRES a fresh 'vite build' first, and every FE change thereafter needs a rebuild; dev mode was a documented deliberate choice for HMR, so scope the switch to demo/prod serving windows exactly as the finding proposes. (2) Verify 'vite build' still succeeds on the current tree before flipping the unit (last known-good build is 2 days old). (3) /models/DG_v1.glb 404s in BOTH modes today (no public/models/) — pre-existing, not a regression from the switch, but will show as a console error either way; public/media/ and fonts are copied into dist/ so the :8890 media path is unaffected.
verify_notes: Mechanism verified line-by-line: ops/v48-web.service ExecStart = 'npx vite --host --port 5188' (dev mode), enabled and running since Jul 12 (PID 3022); the unit's own comment says 'Dev-mode by design today' and pre-writes the exact preview switch the finding proposes. Eager glob confirmed at host/web/src/cmd/registry/fill-loader.ts:9 (finding cited :10; glob is :9), plus two more eager barrels (cmd/prim/index.ts:12, cmd/components/index.ts:8). Exactly 211 static @cmd-v2 imports — matches to the digit. dist/ (Jul 12 08:24, 2.63MB assets, AdminApp code-split + React.lazy in main.tsx) exists unserved. Numbers independently reproduced with a fresh Playwright browser: dev :5188 load event 5,609ms / DCL 5,474ms with the resource-timing buffer capped at exactly 250 entries (231 .ts/.tsx modules) — true request count is >=250, so the claim may undercount. Existing dist/ served statically on a temp port: load event 113ms, FCP 148ms, 13 requests, 2.52MB. No constraint violations: serving-config only, CMD_V2 stays read-only, no per-card code, suite untouched. Vite 5.4.21: preview.proxy defaults to server.proxy, so /api, /copilot, /admin/api proxies carry into 'vite preview' as-is. One sub-claim is overstated: 'frees dev-server CPU during runs' — systemd shows the vite unit consumed only 1min 45s CPU over ~38h; transform CPU only competes during a simultaneous page load and the pipeline's dominant latency is GPU-side vLLM, so treat the CPU-freeing benefit as negligible.

title: RunningCard is a dead spinner for up to 11 minutes — a file-backed progress poll costs nothing
scenario: any run; worst = multi-asset p90 683s
evidence: App.tsx shows static 'Building your real-time view…' for the whole run (p50 42s healthy, RESPONSE_MULTI p90 683s live). The per-stage records (PROMPT/1a/1b/validate/L2.card/exec) are ALREADY streamed to outputs/logs/pipeline_<rid>.jsonl during the run, and run_id is deterministic from the prompt (make_run_id), so the FE can poll progress without touching the pipeline.
current_cost: 42-683s of zero-feedback wait (perceived, not wall-clock)
proposal: GET /api/progress?run_id= reading the tail of pipeline_<rid>.jsonl -> FE ticker ('routing page… authoring card 3/5… filling data…'). Zero pipeline change; superseded later by S1 events.
est_saving: 0s wall-clock; converts the entire wait into visible progress (standard perceived-latency mitigation; users abandon less)
refined_saving: 0s wall-clock (as claimed — this is purely perceived latency). Converts a 41s-median dead wait (p90 143s single; multi tail 4-23 min wall observed, 7.4 min max by clean elapsed_ms) into stage-level progress for every run. Refinement: for multi-asset runs — the longest waits — a naive tail of pipeline_<rid>.jsonl shows only start/end; the endpoint must also tail the per-class lane files (salt 'class:<cls>') to give mid-run ticks there, which is still server-side-only and zero pipeline change. No overlap double-count with wall-clock fixes; explicitly superseded by S1 streaming events later — build it as the cheap stopgap it claims to be.
risk_notes: Low risk confirmed: read-only endpoint + FE polling; no pipeline writes; response contract untouched. Watch-outs: (1) tail-from-last-PROMPT or a repeat prompt instantly shows the previous execution's completed stages (stale-progress bug); (2) multi envelope file has no mid-run records — without lane-file globbing the ticker looks frozen precisely on the worst runs, worse UX than no ticker; (3) polling loop must stop on run completion/error to respect the server.py:418 bursty-connection budget; (4) stage() writes are fail-open (bare except) — the ticker must degrade gracefully to the current static message when the file is absent, never block render.
verify_notes: Mechanism verified line-by-line. (1) App.tsx:233-246 RunningCard is a static spinner + 'Building your real-time view...' shown at line 151 while `loading && !resolving`, for the whole duration of ONE blocking POST /api/run (api.ts:135 — plain await fetch, no streaming/polling/timeout). (2) obs/stage.py:12-20 appends each stage record to outputs/logs/pipeline_<rid>.jsonl per call (open-append-close, immediately visible to a concurrent reader); sampled recent files show PROMPT/1a/1b/validate/L2.card/exec records spanning 26.6-613.9s — genuinely streamed during the run. (3) run/run_id.py make_run_id = sha1('|'+prompt)[:10], deterministic; admin/replay.py _predict_run_id already predicts it the same way (precedent). (4) Host is ThreadingHTTPServer (server.py:30,417) so a concurrent GET /api/progress is servable mid-run. Numbers re-derived from 634 pipeline logs: single e2e (PROMPT->RESPONSE, n=1749) p50=41.0s — claimed 42s checks out; p90=143s, max=440s. Multi: RESPONSE_MULTI elapsed_ms real-run p90=233s (last-3d 366s), max=441.7s; full wall PROMPT->RESPONSE_MULTI pairing (n=18) p50=200s, p90=1044s, max=1377s. The exact 'p90 683s' is not reproducible from disk but sits inside the band between the two measures; 'up to 11 minutes' is supported (conservative by wall-pairing, slightly high by elapsed_ms). No constraint violations: FE + one read-only host endpoint, zero pipeline change, no per-card logic, CMD_V2 untouched, single-asset response bytes untouched, trivially suite-green. Four implementation caveats (refinements, not refutations): (a) MULTI runs — the envelope file pipeline_<make_run_id(prompt)>.jsonl holds ONLY PROMPT+RESPONSE_MULTI (multi_asset.py:102-105 comment states this; per-stage detail lives in salted lane files make_run_id(prompt, salt='class:<cls>'), harness.py:412) — so the WORST-wait case the finding cites needs lane-file discovery server-side, not a literal single-file tail; natural-compare preflight (server.py:399 natural_compare_ids) also runs before the envelope PROMPT record. (b) Deterministic rid + append mode: repeat prompts share one file — the endpoint must tail from the LAST PROMPT record (admin runs.executions() splitter already solves exactly this; reuse it). (c) Reflect attempts 2+ log under salt='loopN' (harness.py:114) — progress appears stalled during reflect unless globbed; rare/cosmetic. (d) Picker flows (pinned re-run, picker-launched compare) render the AssetResolution popup (resolving=true), NOT RunningCard — same zero-feedback problem but the ticker must also feed that popup. FE run_id prediction: prefer passing the prompt to the endpoint and computing make_run_id server-side (crypto.subtle SHA-1 is unavailable on non-localhost http origins). Effort 'small' holds; risk low.

title: Knowledge-gate LLM call is serial before every fresh prompt's pipeline
scenario: every fresh dashboard prompt
evidence: Measured 336ms in trace A (baseline ~0.7s). For dashboard-shaped prompts (the majority) the gate's answer is discarded and 1a||1b only starts after it returns.
current_cost: +0.3-0.7s p50 on every fresh prompt
proposal: Fire the gate and 1a||1b concurrently; abandon the pipeline lane if kind=knowledge (by 0.7s only 1b's first DB probe has run). The gate still decides — only the waiting overlaps.
est_saving: -0.3-0.7s p50 on every fresh dashboard prompt
refined_saving: -0.17s p50 / -0.29s p95 per fresh dashboard prompt (obs-measured dashboard-verdict gate latency, n=310), NOT -0.3-0.7s. Saving is fully realizable since 1a||1b p50 is multi-second, so the 166ms hides entirely under the pipeline lane; nothing new becomes the bottleneck. Against e2e p50 37.8s this is <0.5% — worthwhile only as part of a bundled pre-pipeline overlap.
risk_notes: OVERLAP WARNING: natural_compare_ids (host/server.py:398-402) is ALSO a serial pre-pipeline step on every fresh prompt (runs 1b-resolver candidate probes). Any sibling finding that overlaps the whole pre-pipeline segment (gate + natural-compare) with 1a||1b shares this saving — do not sum them. Also the gate call and 1a would then hit vLLM :8200 concurrently; at 2 small concurrent calls contention is negligible (L2 fan-out already runs 4), but sweeps at high page-concurrency could re-inflate gate p95.
verify_notes:
MECHANISM VERIFIED. host/server.py:370-392: every fresh prompt (asset_id is None and no asset_ids) runs knowledge.ems.ask() — ONE guided-JSON LLM call to :8200 (knowledge/ems.py:82-100, stage='knowledge_ems') — strictly serially; a 'dashboard' verdict discards the answer and only then falls through to the natural_compare_ids preflight (server.py:398-402) and build_response -> run_pipeline, where 1a||1b start concurrently via run_parallel (run/harness.py:241-244). knowledge.enabled='on' live in app_config; gate spans fire daily (obs store, 2026-07-12..07-14). Fail-open wrapper means no correctness coupling — the pipeline never consumes the gate output on the dashboard path.

NUMBERS RE-DERIVED — the claimed cost is 2-4x OVERSTATED. Authoritative source: cmd_catalog.obs_stage_events (stage='knowledge_gate', per-span latency_ms), 317 spans split by verdict: dashboard (n=310, the wasted ones): p50=166ms, mean=216ms, p95=285ms, max=3.4s; stable across days (p50 167/163/162ms on 07-12/13/14). knowledge verdicts p50=1142ms but those are TERMINAL (answer served, nothing wasted). The finding's 0.3-0.7s band came from the profiler's mined knowledge_gate aggregate (outputs/latency/summary.json: median 686ms, avg 794ms) which pools knowledge-answer generations and older router+answer two-call-era samples ('quantized_s' mined records); trace A's 336ms sits at ~p97 of the dashboard-verdict distribution, not p50.

CONSTRAINTS: no violation — AI-first intact (gate still decides), per-leaf honesty untouched, CMD_V2 read-only untouched, single-asset dashboard response byte-identical (gate output was already discarded). Implementation risks (small but real, so 'effort: small / risk: low' is slightly optimistic): (1) ai_log.set_run_id + obs trace contextvars must propagate into the concurrent lane or the ai-attribution leak the log audit fixed regresses (server.py:371-381 comment); (2) the speculative pipeline lane writes its PROMPT stage record before the verdict is known — for a knowledge/off_scope prompt that pollutes the run file, and server.py:376-377 documents the executions splitter requires the file to START at PROMPT; the abandon path must suppress/scrub that; (3) call_qwen can't be cancelled mid-flight, so knowledge prompts (7/317 = ~2% of fresh prompts) burn a wasted 1a call + 1b DB probes on shared vLLM — negligible at that rate.

title: After a picker pick the finished dashboard hides behind a manual 'Open dashboard' click
scenario: every picker interaction
evidence: App.tsx sets resolving=true whenever assetId != null, keeping the AssetResolution popup over the ALREADY-RENDERED grid until the user clicks 'Open dashboard'.
current_cost: +1-3s human reaction + 1 click per picker flow, after already waiting 13-35s for leg2
proposal: Auto-dismiss the popup on a successful pinned result (cards.length>0 && !asset_pending), optionally with a brief 'resolved to X' toast. ~5 FE lines.
est_saving: -1-3s perceived per picker interaction
refined_saving: -1-3s + 1 click per single-asset picker completion for an attentive user; UNBOUNDED (tens of seconds to minutes) when the user tabs away during the 13-35s leg2 wait and never sees the button flip — auto-dismiss recovers that attention loss, so 1-3s is a floor, not a ceiling. Scope: single-pick flows only (multi-asset compare already bypasses via isMulti). Frequency: picker opens on ~22% of fresh prompts (67/303 in obs corpus), though recorded completions are rare (7 pinned re-runs) because most obs traffic is automated sweeps that never pick — per human picker interaction the saving is real. No overlap with any server-side leg2/l2_emit fix: this is terminal-stage perceived latency, purely additive to pipeline speedups, and nothing else becomes the bottleneck (the click is the last serialized step before reveal).
risk_notes: The 'resolved → Open dashboard' state is explicitly documented deliberate design (App.tsx:166 comment 'Pick → re-run pinned → resolved → Open dashboard reveals the cards' and AssetResolution.tsx implements the four-state resolution card from Command Center.dc.html) — auto-dismiss is a product-behavior change that should get an owner nod; the suggested 'resolved to X' toast preserves the confirmation intent. Implementation must auto-dismiss ONLY on terminal success (cards.length>0 && !asset_pending && !asset_no_data && !validation_blocked) — cards.length>0 alone already covers the gated cases since they never reach Layer 2. Do not dismiss during loading (the spinner view is the only leg2 progress feedback). Latent pre-existing edge (unchanged by this fix, worth knowing): a pinned re-run that returns validation_blocked leaves the component's internal view stuck on 'resolved' showing 'Open dashboard' with zero cards behind it — clicking lands on the DataUnavailable outage screen; auto-dismiss neither fixes nor worsens this.
verify_notes: Mechanism verified line-by-line. App.tsx:98 sets resolving=true on EVERY run() completion where assetId != null, so a successful pinned re-run keeps the AssetResolution popup up; AssetResolution.tsx:263 gates the reveal behind an enabled 'Open dashboard' button whose onOpenDashboard (App.tsx:177 setResolving(false)) is the ONLY success-path exit. One correction to the evidence text: the grid is NOT 'already-rendered' behind the popup — hasCards (App.tsx:116) includes !resolving, so CardGrid mounts only AFTER the click; the dashboard DATA is ready in state, but reaction + click + first grid mount are serialized after leg2 today, which slightly strengthens the finding (auto-dismiss overlaps the mount too). Cost context verified from obs_traces (cmd_catalog): 67/303 fresh runs (22%) returned asset_pending=true (picker opens), and the 7 recorded pinned re-runs ran 13.2-34.9s recently (p50 22.2s) — matching the claimed 13-35s leg2 wait. The 1-3s reaction figure is not instrumented (FE has no click telemetry) but the mandatory extra click is unambiguous in code and 1-3s is a conservative attended-user estimate. Constraints: FE-only change in host/web (v48's own shell, CMD_V2 untouched), no AI/per-card hardcode, no per-leaf/byte-contract impact; grep shows NO tests or SSR harness reference the popup flow (only App.tsx, AssetResolution.tsx, a comment in layer1b/resolve/no_data_gate.py), so suite-green is safe. Proposal condition is sound: cards.length>0 && !asset_pending naturally excludes validation_blocked/asset_no_data pinned re-runs (those gate before Layer 2 and return zero cards), and isMulti already skips the popup. Keep the loading-phase 'resolved' spinner view (useful progress UX during the 13-35s leg2); only auto-dismiss the terminal enabled-button state.

title: No gzip on :8770 JSON responses (8-11x compressible, 490KB worst)
scenario: multi-asset response over VPN/WAN client
evidence: Measured response_*.json: largest 490KB/464KB/454KB; gzip ratios 8.0x/5.4x/10.9x (490KB->61KB). _send always writes identity encoding; Vite proxy does not compress.
current_cost: ~30-40ms LAN; 0.3-3s for any VPN/remote client on the biggest responses
proposal: Honor Accept-Encoding with stdlib gzip in _send (~5 lines), or front :8770 with Caddy. Also shrinks the FE JSON.parse input.
est_saving: -30-40ms LAN / -0.3-3s WAN on large responses; free
refined_saving: Per-response, network-leg only: ~34ms on 100Mbps LAN, ~170ms-2.3s on 5-20Mbps/1.5Mbps VPN for the 490KB worst case (p90 190KB scales to ~13ms LAN / 65-890ms VPN; p50 64KB ~4ms LAN / ~90ms at 5Mbps); minus 2-5ms gzip CPU. ~0 saving for a localhost/dev browser on the same box. Effort genuinely small (~10 lines in _send honoring Accept-Encoding).
risk_notes: Overlaps: (1) any payload-slimming finding (wire-field pruning, echoed-defaults trimming) — savings not additive, gzip compresses whatever remains; (2) any reverse-proxy/Caddy adoption elsewhere in the audit — same fix, count once; (3) if a streaming/SSE partial-results finding lands, transport moves to a new code path and gzip must be re-applied there. Perspective: <0.1% of e2e p50 37.8s pipeline latency — value is purely last-leg perceived latency for remote/VPN clients; compute (l2_emit) stays the bottleneck. Implementation risks: compressed Content-Length must be exact for HTTP/1.1 keep-alive; only compress on explicit Accept-Encoding: gzip; drop the incorrect JSON.parse-input rationale from the writeup.
verify_notes: Mechanism verified in code: host/server.py:198-207 _send() emits identity-encoded JSON with explicit Content-Length; no gzip/Accept-Encoding/Content-Encoding handling anywhere in server.py (grep), and host/web/vite.config.ts proxies /api via plain http-proxy with no compression layer. Server is ThreadingHTTPServer bound 0.0.0.0:8770 and Vite binds host:true, so the browser leg crosses the client's real network. Numbers re-derived from real artifacts: 403 outputs/logs/response_*.json, p50 64KB / p90 190KB / max 490,105B; measured gzip level-6 on the 490KB body = 61,297B (8.0x) costing 5.0ms CPU (level-1: 6.2x in 1.8ms). Transfer arithmetic matches the claim: 100Mbps LAN save 34ms; 20Mbps VPN 172ms; 5Mbps 686ms; 1.5Mbps 2.29s. Proposal violates no constraint: transport-only, decompressed bytes identical (byte-identical contract intact); compress only when the client sends Accept-Encoding so raw http.client tests keep identity and requests-based tests transparently decompress — suite-safe. Implementation notes: set Content-Length to the compressed length, skip bodies under ~1KB and the 204 OPTIONS path. One sub-claim is wrong: gzip does NOT shrink FE JSON.parse input (browser decompresses first; parse input byte-identical) — only wire transfer shrinks.

title: /api/site LIVE probe hits :5433 per poll per client (15s header + 12s outage cadence)
scenario: outage/tunnel-flap periods; many open tabs
evidence: Each poll executes q(DATA_DB,'SELECT 1') live; during a tunnel flap each hangs a handler thread up to the connect timeout; N tabs multiply it (4-5 probes/min each).
current_cost: minor healthy (1-15ms/probe); handler-thread + tunnel burn during flaps
proposal: Server-side memoized liveness (TTL 5-10s) shared across clients + a tight connect/statement timeout for this probe only.
est_saving: negligible wall-clock; removes probe pileup and thread burn during outages
refined_saving: Zero on prompt->cards e2e latency (background polling, off the user-perceived path; e2e p50 37.8s is l2_emit-dominated). Healthy state: ~10-12ms server work x 4/min x N tabs eliminated — negligible, as claimed. Real value is outage-mode resource burn: today N tabs x 4-9 probes/min each hold a handler thread up to ~5s (fresh-connect cap) or minutes (half-open pooled socket, unbounded read) and each burn a tunnel connect attempt; with a 5-10s server-side TTL this collapses to <=6-12 DB attempts/min TOTAL independent of tab count, i.e. with 5 tabs roughly 25-45 blocked-thread events/min -> ~6-12/min, and thread pileup bounded to ~1 in-flight probe. Effort small (one memoized helper around the existing q() call), risk low.
risk_notes: No overlap with the big wall-clock fixes (l2_emit, per-card LLM latency) — this is orthogonal outage-hygiene, so its saving neither double-counts nor unblocks another bottleneck. Two cautions: (1) if only connect_timeout is relied on, the half-open pooled-socket mode still blocks one probe thread arbitrarily long — pair the TTL with a bounded statement/socket timeout on the probe path or the memoized probe itself can go stale-in-flight; (2) keep TTL <= ~10s or the header LIVE dot lags real recovery/outage noticeably against its 12-15s poll cadence; (3) caching live=False must expire on TTL, not be pinned (the panel_members cache-poison lesson inverted: here negatives are cacheable but must stay short-lived).
verify_notes: Mechanism verified line-for-line: host/server.py /api/site runs q(DATA_DB,'SELECT 1') live per GET (DATA_DB = target_version1 over :5433 tunnel, config/databases.py _TUNNEL_DBS); pollers are CommandHeader.tsx:19 useSiteStatus(15000) (always mounted) + DataUnavailable.tsx:12 useSiteStatus(12000) (outage view only, single mount at App.tsx:135) = 4/min healthy, ~9/min per tab in outage view. Server is ThreadingHTTPServer (server.py:417, daemon_threads, unbounded) so each hung probe holds a handler thread. Healthy cost re-measured live: 10-12ms full HTTP round-trip x5 curls — matches claimed 1-15ms. Flap cost: pg_connect connect_timeout=5 (db_client.py:240) caps the fresh-connect attempt at ~5s, and the pool self-heal (stale-fail -> discard -> one fresh connect -> raise) means each probe can hold a thread ~5s+; the finding is actually UNDERSTATED for the half-open-socket mode: an established pooled connection has no socket/statement timeout, so the SELECT 1 read on a stale socket can block on TCP retransmission far beyond 5s before the retry even starts. No constraint violations: TTL memoization is host-layer infra, no per-card hardcode, no CMD_V2 writes, no leaf semantics; LIVE dot staleness grows <=10s vs the 12-15s FE cadence; data/ttl_cache.py is direct precedent; no tests pin /api/site. Implementation nuance: caching live=False IS desired here (collapsing outage probes is the point) — do not copy ttl_cache's never-cache-empty rule for this consumer. Also worth adding a socket-level guard (e.g. statement_timeout or options='-c statement_timeout=2000' on the probe, or probe over a dedicated fresh connect) so the half-open case is bounded too.

title: VERIFICATION: admin stage '1a' = max(1a,1b) join and EXCLUDES the pre-flight gap — console understates user-perceived latency
scenario: latency accounting for all scenarios
evidence: The '1a' stage record is written after run_parallel joins BOTH lanes, so admin p50 9.6s/p90 120s/max 415s is dominated by 1b has_data probes (route LLM itself measured 0.5s, stories 1.3-1.6s, page_selection 0.5-1.0s). The PROMPT record is stamped inside run_pipeline, AFTER the natural_compare gap — admin misses up to 188s p90 of real user wait; obs request_received is the honest clock.
current_cost: n/a (accounting) — but it hides the S3 186s tail from the ops console
proposal: Split the stage records ('1a_route' vs '1b_resolve') and stamp PROMPT (or a 'received' record) at handle_run entry so the console clock matches the user clock.
est_saving: 0s directly; prevents mis-prioritized optimization and makes S3's win visible
refined_saving: 0s direct (accounting fix) — as claimed. What it unhides, quantified: (a) inside console '1a' p50 9.6s, ~6-7s is the 1b lane (basket LLM p50 6.4s + has_data probes), not the route (0.5s) — redirects optimization from routing to 1b resolution; (b) a wholly invisible pre-flight gap of p50 1.8s / p90 187s / max 539s (fresh prompts p90 188.5s) between request receipt and the PROMPT stamp — the natural_compare/S3 tail. No overlap with other fixes' savings; it is the measurement precondition for S3 and any 1b-probe fix. Smaller implementation than proposed: the honest per-lane numbers ALREADY exist in obs_stage_events (page_selection/story_selection/asset_resolution spans with latency_ms, plus request_received) — pointing admin/latency.py at obs spans instead of pairwise stagelog gaps avoids touching the stagelog format at all.
risk_notes: The proposal as written has two implementation traps: (1) admin/runs.py executions() starts a new slice at each PROMPT record — a 'received' record stamped at handle_run entry appends BEFORE PROMPT in pipeline_<rid>.jsonl and would land at the tail of the PREVIOUS execution slice (mis-attributing the pre-flight wait to the prior run's last stage) unless the splitter also treats 'received' as a slice boundary; host/server.py ~line 376 documents the 'file must still START at PROMPT' invariant for the executions splitter. (2) Stage lines are END-markers paired by ts gaps — parallel lanes cannot be represented by splitting/reordering records; each lane must log its own measured elapsed as an explicit field, or the console must read obs_stage_events latency_ms (recommended). Also update admin/config.py's canonical stage vocabulary if new stage names are introduced, and note conftest re-pins run ids per test (console-entry-validation), so stagelog-format changes need a suite pass. Risk otherwise low: read-path/telemetry only.
verify_notes: Mechanism verified in code and numbers verified against live data. (1) harness.py:241-244 run_parallel (home lib/parallel.py) joins BOTH lanes via fut.result() before returning; the '1a' stage record (harness.py:280) and '1b' record (line 285) are stamped back-to-back AFTER the join. admin/latency.py:30-36 derives duration as pairwise ts gaps within an execution slice, so console '1a' = PROMPT->join = max(1a_route, 1b_resolve) and '1b' collapses to ~0 — live admin :8790 confirms: 1a n=2312 p50=9582ms p90=120466ms max=415418ms (exactly the claimed 9.6s/120s/415s), 1b n=2043 avg=0 max=11ms. (2) Decomposition confirmed from obs_llm_calls: route p50=472ms/p90=772ms, stories p50=1536ms, page_selection span p50=469ms/p90=754ms — vs the 1b lane: basket LLM p50=6383ms/p90=16566ms, asset_resolution span p50=9129ms/p90=179316ms/max=371669ms. The 1a-lane LLM work is ~2-3s; the 9.6s p50 / 120s p90 console '1a' is 1b-resolve dominated, as claimed. (3) Pre-PROMPT gap confirmed from obs pg (request_received span -> legacy.PROMPT event): n=311 p50=1782ms p90=187204ms max=538597ms; fresh-prompt (knowledge-gate) subset n=302 p90=188515ms — the claimed 'up to 188s p90' verified nearly exactly. knowledge_ems LLM is tiny (p50=166ms/p90=271ms), so the gap is natural_compare_ids (host/server.py:399, runs the full 1b resolver per named asset incl. has_data probes) plus the multi-lane shared route; RESPONSE_MULTI is a single-record execution so it drops out of the stage table entirely (latency.py docstring admits this). Profiler corroborates: outputs/latency/summary.json mined route_resolve_wall n=1284 median 8.3s p95 125s max 239s. (4) Constraint check: telemetry-only, no AI-first/per-leaf/CMD_V2/byte-contract impact. Caveat on magnitude: the tails (415s '1a' max, 539s gap max) are partly sweep/vLLM-contention artifacts from validation-campaign load, so interactive-user p90 is likely lower than the mined figures — but the mis-attribution and the invisible pre-flight gap are real at every load level.

title: Single shared neuract connection serializes the entire 8-way card fan-out (and /api/frame bursts)
scenario: cold 5-8-card panel-overview page; /api/frame date-change burst
evidence: data/neuract_pool.py:27 `_POOL` holds exactly ONE psycopg2 conn per DSN key; run_read (:80-96) executes on it from all 8 exec threads (host/exec_cards.py:250 max_workers<=8). Proof from obs: trace t_10ca417759b44007b72f9e900e1cc2dd — 4 panel cards all started 08:55:19.665, summed card walls 48.4+34.5+32.0+26.6=141.5s EXACTLY equals summed neuract query latency 141.6s (1204 queries); per-query med 58ms contended vs 25ms measured uncontended (16ms tunnel RTT + 8.7ms exec); executor parent span clipped at the 45s budget (45001ms).
current_cost: 4-card panel page exec wall ~45s (budget-clipped; real query total 141s); frame POSTs queue behind runs
proposal: Replace the 1-conn dict with psycopg2 ThreadedConnectionPool (min 2, max ~8-12, size >= exec thread pool), keeping DSN/keepalives/drop-broken semantics; flag-gate (e.g. V48_DB_POOL_N) for rollback.
est_saving: -28s exec wall on the 4-panel-card page (wall goes from sum-of-all-cards' queries ~45s to slowest-card ~17s pre-batching: 686q x 25ms); also unblocks EXEC-6/7 parallelism
refined_saving: -25s exec wall on the 4-panel-card page (45s budget-clipped, true 48.4s -> ~20s: slowest card 643q x ~30ms measured-solo + non-DB overhead; claim's 17s used 25ms/q which is slightly optimistic). Plus a correctness win: card 14 goes from budget-blanked (data fetched then discarded at 45s) to real ~20s. Scales to 5-8-card panel pages (worse today: more threads on one conn). /api/frame de-queueing claim directionally confirmed (same readonly=False pool key).
risk_notes: Low. Keep per-(readonly,dsn) sub-pools so the registries door's readonly session flag survives; keep drop-broken-conn + never-cache-empty semantics; pool max 8-12 per key means up to ~20-24 conns through the :5433 tunnel to archbox PG — verify max_connections headroom (default 100, shared with other services). SSH tunnel multiplexes channels over one TCP conn — fine at this payload size, but a tunnel flap now breaks N conns at once so drop/reconnect must be per-connection (ThreadedConnectionPool putconn(close=True) on error). Flag-gate (V48_DB_POOL_N) as proposed for rollback. Saving overlaps EXEC-6/7 batching — count only one.
verify_notes: Mechanism verified in code today: data/neuract_pool.py:27 _POOL = one psycopg2 conn per (readonly, dsn) key; run_read(:80-96) executes on it from all exec threads (host/exec_cards.py:250 max_workers<=8); psycopg2's connection lock serializes concurrent execute(). Both facades call only run_read (no held cursors/session state beyond the readonly pool key), so a ThreadedConnectionPool swap is drop-in. Numbers re-derived from cmd_catalog obs tables for trace t_10ca417759b44007b72f9e900e1cc2dd: card walls 48.39+34.45+32.02+26.62=141.5s all starting 08:55:19.665; executor.card neuract queries 1192q/139.0s = 98.2% of card walls (claim's 1204q/141.6s reproduces exactly when adding layer2_card_ai.card 8q/1.8s + metadata_resolution 4q/0.7s). Decisive contention proof the claimant didn't have: card 14's per-query avg on its OWN query stream scales with active-card count — 186.5ms (4 cards) -> 95.4ms (2-3 cards) -> 31.3ms (solo after 08:55:54). Live tunnel RTT measured 11-15ms (SELECT 1 over :5433), so uncontended ~30ms/query (claim's 25ms slightly optimistic). Bonus defect confirmed: executor parent span clipped at 45001ms while card 14 finished at 48.4s — its fetched payload is discarded as 'executor budget exceeded' today, so the fix also converts a budget-blanked card to real. Constraint check: pure infra change, no per-card hardcode (AI-first ok), degrade semantics unchanged (NeuractReadError/drop-broken preserved per proposal), CMD_V2 untouched, no data-shape change (byte-identical contract safe). Overlap/next-bottleneck: post-pool the wall = slowest card's own serialized round-trips (card 14: 643q x ~30ms ~= 19.5s) — i.e. round-trip COUNT becomes the lever, which is EXEC-6/7 territory; if batching lands first, this fix's absolute saving shrinks (and vice versa) — do not sum the two savings. Also: this trace's e2e contained 176.6s of serial pre-executor fullness-probe queries (8q, 15-28s each, 08:51:48-08:54:45, the known all-or-nothing fullness probe) — exec-wall saving must not be sold as e2e saving.

title: member_delta issues 4 sequential endpoint queries per member per delta key — batchable to ~4 statements per slot-window
scenario: panel-power card 14 (28-member panel), any energy/entries panel card
evidence: energy_registers.py:62-83 member_delta = member_delta_pair(import)+member_delta_pair(export), each _nx.window(tbl,[col]) = 2 queries (neuract.py:175-207) though window() already accepts a column LIST. Card 14 recipe (cmd_catalog card_fill_recipe): scalar+entries+entries slots each with delta elements over range this-month + prepare_ctx panel_kwh over run window -> predicted ~672 endpoint queries; observed span 2a6684b057f9: 686 queries, 616 single-column first/last endpoint reads (308 ASC + 308 DESC, med 33-40ms, p90 190-210ms) = 48.5s of the 52.7s card wall. Measured: UNION ALL of 28 tables x (first+last) in ONE statement = 165ms vs 971ms as 56 sequential queries.
current_cost: worst card 52.7s; panel_aggregate p50 6.1s with median 106 queries (n=457 spans)
proposal: (a) read both paired registers in ONE window() call (4->2 q/member); (b) prepare-time gather of ALL roster delta columns per window -> one first+last pair per member for the full colset; (c) UNION ALL across the 28 members -> ~2 statements per (window,colset), ~4-8 per card. Recipes/AI emissions untouched.
est_saving: -40s+ on the worst panel-power card (616q x 40ms ~ 24.6s contended -> ~4-8 stmts x 165ms ~ 1s; 52.7s -> <5s); panel_aggregate p50 6.1s -> ~1.5s
refined_saving: Worst panel-power card (card 14): 52.7s -> ~6-9s with batching alone (4-7 UNION statements x ~1.0s each, ::timestamptz cast kept) = -44 to -47s; -> ~2-4s only when combined with index-friendly ORDER BY (raw text column) or an expression index (~0.2s/statement). The headline '<5s' therefore needs BOTH components. panel_aggregate p50: 6.1s -> realistic ~2-3s (claim of ~1.5s is the optimistic edge; bucketed/series trend reads, latest-row reads and per-card residue are untouched by this fix). Page executor wall (4 concurrent panel cards on the shared single conn): ~53s -> ~3-8s since the same machinery serves all panel cards.
risk_notes: (1) The 165ms measurement silently bundles a second change: ORDER BY raw timestamp_utc text instead of the ::timestamptz cast. Lexicographic==chronological verified on sampled gic tables (fixed-width ISO, fixed +05:30 offset) but must be audited fleet-wide, or replaced by expression indexes on the live neuract logging DB (already an R-backlog item '::timestamptz index' — if another finding claims that index, savings overlap heavily: index alone gets sequential reads to ~641 x 16ms RTT ~ 10s, batching alone ~6-9s, both ~2-4s; do not double-count). (2) UNION ALL legs need uniform SELECT lists — per-table absent columns require NULL placeholders to preserve honest-null padding. (3) Replay tapes (per-door tape kinds) and property suite fixtures encode today's SQL shapes; expect fixture regeneration. (4) Post-fix bottleneck shifts: layer2_card_ai LLM emit (25s in this trace, serial before executor) becomes dominant — e2e page saving is capped at the executor share (~45-53s -> ~3-8s), it does not compound with LLM-side findings. (5) Equal-timestamp tie-break between ASC/DESC legs is unchanged-risk (already ambiguous today).
