est_saving: -0.1s p50; -up to 28s on tail runs.

title: Validate's pandas probe: un-indexed ORDER BY ts::timestamptz DESC LIMIT 500 over the tunnel is the 119s validate tail
evidence: PROBE_ROWS=500; ORDER BY the TEXT ts column cast to timestamptz = full sort per probe on 100k-900k-row gic_* tables over :5433. Baseline validate p50 312ms / max 119s. It IS a true pre-L2 dep (fold_into_basket feeds the L2 gates) so it can only be made cheaper or earlier, not removed.
current_cost: 0.3s p50, up to 119s tail, serial between 1a||1b join and L2
proposal: TTL-cache the (table, column-set) probe result with the same cache.resolution_ttl_s staleness contract as has_data; kick the probe off inside the 1b lane as soon as the basket is built (overlaps granularity_reconcile + 1a tail). DB lens: recent-window WHERE bound or expression index.
est_saving: -0.3s p50 when cached; kills the 119s tail.

title: Response-tail synchronous persistence + double serialization before the body is sent
evidence: _dump_response json.dump (490KB measured, 2.8ms + write) and replay write_bundle (bundle median ~0.7MB, max 4.5MB events.jsonl, written file-by-file) both complete BEFORE _send re-serializes the same dict (json.dumps 2.8ms measured) and writes the socket. Applies to every /api/run AND /api/frame.
current_cost: ~15-55ms per request of avoidable pre-send IO
proposal: Serialize once (reuse bytes for dump + wire); move _dump_response and write_bundle to a fire-and-forget thread after the socket write (both are fail-open by design already).
est_saving: -15-55ms every request; most visible on /api/frame date-control re-fetches where total is ~1-3s.

title: elapsed_ms and admin stage numbers hide/mislabel the real wait — pre-pipeline serve work is invisible
evidence: response.elapsed_ms starts at build_response t0: run r_48c83f28a2 reported 111.4s of a 296s true wait; RESPONSE_MULTI reported 55.3s of 229.7s. Admin '1a' (avg 26.9s p90 120s) is actually the PROMPT->1a ts-gap = the 1a||1b JOIN (1b probes dominate) — answering the brief's VERIFY. 'notes' includes payload_final; 'validate' includes granularity_reconcile's 1a re-run.
current_cost: 0ms directly, but every past optimization decision was made on numbers missing the largest component (the 185s pre-flight)
proposal: Stamp t_request at handle_run entry; emit stage records for knowledge_gate and natural_compare with their own ts; compute elapsed_ms from t_request. Zero runtime cost.
est_saving: 0ms directly; prerequisite for tracking every other fix honestly.

title: Concurrent prompts multiply vLLM emit pressure with no host-level admission control
evidence: :8770 is ThreadingHTTPServer (backlog 128, daemon threads) — verified, so requests never queue on accept; but each run independently applies layer2.emit_concurrency=4, so 2 concurrent prompts = 8 concurrent ~22K-token emits splitting vLLM decode throughput (the documented contention that pushed the harmonics emit to the 150s fail-fast edge). Second-user latency degrades multiplicatively, and RESPONSE_MULTI parallel lanes (HOST-2) would add more.
current_cost: under 2 concurrent prompts, per-card emit p50 ~doubles; timeout-retry cascades on the slowest card
proposal: One process-global emit semaphore (DB knob, e.g. layer2.emit_concurrency_global=6) shared by all requests/lanes, so total in-flight emits stay in vLLM's sweet spot regardless of request count.
est_saving: prevents 2x-4x per-card emit inflation under concurrency (protects the 19.8s p50 per card from becoming 40-60s); required companion to HOST-2 lane parallelism.

title: No gzip on ~0.5MB responses — matters only on VPN/WAN links
evidence: Measured: 490KB response gzips to 61KB in 4.7ms. LAN transfer ~4ms (irrelevant); over a 50Mbps VPN link 490KB = ~78ms vs 10ms gzipped. Response composition (24-card multi): payload 142KB, data_instructions 130KB, render 123KB (per-leaf gap records), refetch 42KB.
current_cost: ~60-70ms per response on VPN links; ~0 on LAN
proposal: Accept-Encoding negotiation in _send (stdlib gzip, 4.7ms); optionally trim data_instructions/render.gaps wire fields the FE never reads (bytes, not ms).
est_saving: -60-70ms per response on remote links only.

title: Verified non-issues / dead ends (do not re-spend effort)
evidence: (1) obs jsonl sink: measured 0.005ms/append -> ~7-10ms per run at ~1,310 db events; pg sink already buffered+background. (2) insight narrator (~1.3s) runs INSIDE the executor fan-out per narrative card, parallel and content-hash cached — no serial host cost. (3) executor budget as_completed(timeout)+shutdown(wait=False,cancel_futures=True)+done-future harvest is correct — no straggler join. (4) enrich/renderer 4ms for 5 cards. (5) _special_handling_map called twice per assemble = 1 redundant ~5ms cmd_catalog read (micro).
current_cost: ~10-25ms per run combined
proposal: Only the trivial dedupe of _special_handling_map if the file is touched anyway; otherwise no action.
est_saving: ~-10ms per run maximum; documented so other lenses skip these.

title: has_data probe (value_counts) ::timestamptz cast defeats the timestamp index -> 195 seq scans = 114-182s
evidence: value_counts probes 314 existing tables in 8 UNION-ALL chunks of 40, each subquery `SELECT to_jsonb(s) FROM tbl s ORDER BY "timestamp_utc"::timestamptz DESC LIMIT 1`. MEASURED cold full call = 181,842ms (dead end warm = 0.1ms, cached 120s). EXPLAIN ANALYZE on pqm_transformer_3 (375k rows): cast -> Parallel Seq Scan + top-N Sort = 2033ms; identical query WITHOUT cast -> Index Scan using idx_..._ts (btree on timestamp_utc text) = 0.064ms (30,000x). A btree index EXISTS on every table's timestamp_utc but casting text->timestamptz makes it unusable. pg_stat: 373 tables, 195 tables >50k rows, 22.6M rows. Proven chunk-of-40: cast=14,508ms vs plain-text index=584ms; projected full value_counts cast=113.9s vs plain=4.6s.
proposal: Drop the ::timestamptz cast in the has_data latest-row probe (value_probe.py:73) so the existing timestamp_utc btree is used. For a has_data non-null-COUNT signal the exact chronological latest is immaterial (ISO text sorts chronologically by date prefix; the value_counts already fail-opens). Keep it probe-local (do NOT change the global DATA_TS_CAST used by genuine time-window math). Flag-gate behind a config knob and verify the single-asset candidate ordering is byte-identical. Optionally parallelize the 8 chunks on the psycopg2 pool for the residual 4.6s.
est_saving: -110 to -177s on any 1b run that triggers a cold/expired value_counts re-probe (114-182s -> 4.6s). This is the p95 200s driver: asset_candidates() runs at the top of EVERY cold resolve, the cache TTL is 120s, so a lightly-loaded server re-probes constantly. Arithmetic: 8 chunks x 14,508ms = 116s cast vs 8 x 584ms = 4.6s plain.
current_cost: 114-182s (measured) on the re-probe request; 0.1ms warm
scenario: cold single-asset dashboard, and every ~120s-TTL re-probe on a running server

title: Persist a has_data snapshot in cmd_catalog (:5432, ~4ms) refreshed by a background cron instead of a live :5433 tunnel probe
file: data/value_probe.py:55-96, data/registry/lt_mfm.py (mirror pattern already used for registry_rows)
evidence: The has_data probe is the ONLY request-time asset-resolution read that still rides the flaky :5433 tunnel (registry_rows/parent_ids already read the local cmd_catalog `registry_*` mirror). Measured tunnel RTT :5433 ~50-64ms vs local :5432 ~4ms (SELECT 1, n=5). value_counts is TTL-cached 120s by frozenset-of-tables, so it re-probes the whole plant every 2 minutes and any tunnel flap during a re-probe raises the honest data_unavailable terminal (asset_resolve.py:105 comment documents this exact contention/outage failure).
proposal: Materialize a `registry_has_data(table_name, value_count, meaningful, refreshed_at)` snapshot table in cmd_catalog, refreshed by the same scripts/sync_neuract_registry.py background job (staleness is fine — neuract appends continuously). tables_with_values reads it from :5432 (~4ms) with a fail-open to the live probe when the snapshot is stale/absent. Removes the tunnel dependency from every asset resolution.
est_saving: -4.6s per re-probe request even after L1B-01 (4.6s tunnel probe -> ~4ms local read), plus eliminates the flap-induced data_unavailable false terminals. Complements L1B-01.
current_cost: 4.6s (post-L1B-01) / 114-182s (pre) per re-probe; tunnel-flap outage risk
scenario: every cold/expired 1b resolve; multi-asset compares that fan several concurrent probes

title: Cache the basket LLM output by (prompt, column-dict fingerprint) — picker re-POST and repeat prompts re-pay 6.4s
stage: 1b column basket / build_basket LLM call stage='basket'
evidence: obs_llm_calls: stage='basket' p50 6,382ms, p95 20,358ms, max 56,536ms (n=308) — the slow LLM in 1b (asset_resolve is p50 579ms). build_basket runs the LLM on EVERY run_1b, even a pinned picker re-POST: pinned_skip (asset_resolve.py:115) skips only resolution, build_basket (build.py:38) still fires. neuract schema is 96% uniform (185/373 tables share the 72-col signature; UPS vs transformer share 70 of ~72-74 cols), so the AI's card-agnostic feasible-column decision is a function of (prompt, column dictionary), identical across sibling meters.
proposal: Add a TTL/process cache of the basket AI result keyed on (normalized_prompt_metric, frozenset(column_name), frozenset(has_data_columns)). This caches the AI's own decision (explicitly allowed — not a per-card hardcode). Hits on: picker re-POST (same prompt, now-pinned asset), repeat/tweaked prompts on the same asset, and cross-class multi-asset compares whose sibling schemas match.
est_saving: -6.4s p50 (up to -20s p95) on the picker re-POST round trip and on repeat prompts; -6.4s per additional distinct class on a multi-class compare (lanes run sequentially, harness.py:409-416).
current_cost: 6.4s p50 / 20.4s p95 per basket build
scenario: picker re-POST round trip, repeat prompts, multi-class compare

title: window_nonnull / latest_nonnull carry the same index-defeating ::timestamptz cast on the resolved table
evidence: window_nonnull (basket's per-column has_data hint) reads `SELECT * FROM tbl ORDER BY "timestamp_utc"::timestamptz DESC LIMIT 20`. EXPLAIN on a 375k-row transformer with cast = Parallel Seq Scan (~2s for the wide 20-row window); on the 85k UPS table measured 30.9ms. Same btree-defeat as L1B-01, but only on the ONE resolved table per run (not all 314).
proposal: Drop the cast here too (index scan gives the latest N rows correctly by date). Same probe-local flag as L1B-01.
est_saving: -up to ~2s on single-asset runs that resolve a large meter (the 8 pqm_transformer_* incomers, 375k rows each); ~0 on small tables.
current_cost: up to ~2s on large-table single-asset runs
scenario: cold single-asset dashboard on a transformer/incomer

title: real_table_cols runs 3x per basket build — 2 redundant information_schema tunnel reads
evidence: real_table_cols is an information_schema.columns query over the :5433 tunnel, MEASURED 63.7ms cold, and is NOT cached. In one build_basket it is called 3 times for the same table: inside window_nonnull (col_dict.py:39), inside _has_electrical->col_dict, and again at column_basket.py:63. 2 of 3 are pure duplicates.
proposal: Memoize real_table_cols per table_name (a small TTLCache like the other resolution caches, or pass the column set through build_basket once). Schema is static per table so staleness is irrelevant.
est_saving: -~120ms per 1b run (2 redundant tunnel info_schema reads x ~60ms).
current_cost: ~190ms (3 x ~63ms) where ~63ms is needed
scenario: every single-asset and per-lane compare 1b run

title: asset_candidates() is built twice per resolve_asset (2nd via class_from_subject->_known_classes)
evidence: resolve_asset calls asset_candidates() at line 110, then class_from_subject(prompt) at line 134 calls asset_candidates() AGAIN via _known_classes() only to compute the distinct-class set. The 2nd build re-runs the uncached _alias_map pcc_panel_alias JOIN and rebuilds 328 python rows (the value probe itself is cache-hit).
proposal: Thread the already-built cands into class_from_subject/_known_classes (it only needs {c[5] for c in cands}). One-line dedup that keeps the class prior identical.
est_saving: -tens of ms per resolve (python rebuild + one redundant pcc_panel_alias query); larger if the value cache is cold.
current_cost: 2x candidate builds per resolve
scenario: every 1b resolve (single + per-lane)

title: Static pcc_panel_alias table (28 rows) queried ~5x uncached per resolve
evidence: pcc_panel_alias (28 rows, static registry facts) is re-read uncached: _alias_map's JOIN (asset_candidates.py:131), _pcc_alias_index (asset_resolve.py:98), and _pcc_section_index twice inside _finish via panel_section+compare_sections (:71,:85). ~5 reads/resolve on :5432 (~4ms each). All static data with no request-varying inputs.
proposal: TTL-cache the three pcc alias index builders (they take no per-request args). Reuses the cache.resolution_ttl_s pattern already in the codebase.
est_saving: -~20-40ms per resolve (5 static reads x ~4ms), more when the 2nd asset_candidates re-fires them.
current_cost: ~20-40ms/resolve
scenario: every 1b resolve, especially PCC-panel prompts

title: asset_resolve stable 5.9K-token listing prefix shows 0% prefix cache (cross-lens note)
evidence: The asset_resolve prompt = asset_system.md (5,620B) + a 328-row listing (17,823 chars); total ~23,661 chars ~= 5,915 tokens. When class_from_subject returns None (common), the listing is the full 328 rows — byte-identical across prompts except the trailing PROMPT line — a stable multi-KB prefix that should hit vLLM prefix cache but journal shows 0.0% always. Low value in THIS lens: asset_resolve LLM is already p50 579ms (n=630). The 0% is a system-wide vLLM issue better owned by the Layer-2 lens.
proposal: Ensure the stable system+listing block is emitted first and verbatim so vLLM prefix caching engages; investigate why prompt-prefix caching is globally 0% (construction adds a per-call varying prefix). Trivial here since the listing is already prompt-invariant when no class prior fires.
est_saving: -~0.3-0.5s per asset_resolve when prefill is cached (5.9K tokens / 10,440 tok/s ~= 0.57s prefill saved on a hit); small vs the 182s and 6.4s levers above.
current_cost: ~0.58s p50 per asset_resolve
scenario: every resolve; larger effect on the whole pipeline via the shared vLLM

title: has_data value probe ORDER BY "timestamp_utc"::timestamptz defeats the ts index — cold asset_candidates() = 182.9s measured
stage: 1b probe on the 1a/1b join (dominates the admin '1a' span)
evidence: EXPLAIN ANALYZE on neuract :5433: with cast -> Seq Scan 6,897 rows + top-N sort = 70.182ms; WITHOUT cast -> Index Scan Backward using idx_..._ts = 0.042ms (1,667x). One 40-table UNION chunk measured 14.5s; full cold asset_candidates() = 182.9s (328 rows), warm = 11ms. Matches obs asset_resolution p90 179,316ms / max 371s and the recomputed PROMPT->'1a' join gap p90 123.9s (n=2,094 from 634 pipeline_*.jsonl). RTT to :5433 is only 16ms — the cost is server-side seq scans.
current_cost: ~183s per cold probe; asset_resolution p90 179s; join gap p90 124s
proposal: Drop the ::timestamptz cast in the probe ORDER BY (timestamp_utc is ISO text: lexicographic == chronological, so the raw-column btree index gives the same latest row), or add expression indexes ((timestamp_utc::timestamptz)) on neuract if writable. Flag-gate; verify one table where format could vary.
est_saving: 183s -> ~2-5s cold probe (8 chunks x (RTT 16ms + 40 index scans x 0.042ms)); ~-120s p90 on cold single-asset dashboards, -170s off asset_resolution p99

title: TTL cache (120s) is shorter than the cold fill (183s), no single-flight, no refresh-ahead — every >2min-idle prompt pays the cold probe serially
evidence: Measured: cold fill 182.9s > TTL 120s; warm 11ms. TTLCache has no lock — N concurrent cold requests each fire the full probe chain (stampede over the VPN tunnel). No background refresh, so interactive traffic (prompts minutes apart) is effectively always cold.
current_cost: up to 183s added to any prompt arriving >120s after the previous one; multiplied under concurrent cold requests
proposal: (a) single-flight lock per cache key; (b) host background refresher re-probing every ~0.8*TTL so caches are warm for the process life (never-cache-empty rule already guards flap poisoning); (c) raise TTL for the value probe specifically. Flag-gated.
est_saving: -180s worst / -7s p50 (9.6s join -> ~2.5s warm path) on cold dashboards today; after L1A-01 still -2-5s p50 cold + stampede elimination

title: run_pipeline_multi executes class lanes sequentially — 3-class compares stack 3 full pipelines
evidence: for cls, members in by_class.items(): lane = run_pipeline(...) — each lane is a full 1b+validate+L2 fan-out run serially; lanes 1..N-1 only need shared_1a (established by lane 0), not each other. Admin: RESPONSE_MULTI p50 142s p90 683s max 835s; slowest runs = 3-feeder compares 410-441s = ~3 x per-lane wall ~140s.
current_cost: N-class compare = N x full-pipeline wall, serial (observed 410-441s for 3)
proposal: Run lane 0 to route the template, then lanes 1..N-1 concurrently via run_parallel with deepcopy(shared_1a) (the injection mechanism already exists); enable the shipped llm.global_concurrency admission knob (llm/client.py:107-130, default 0=off) first to protect :8200.
est_saving: 3-class: 3x140=420s -> 140+max(140,140)=280s = -140s (-33%); conservatively -100s p90 on RESPONSE_MULTI with L2 contention

title: Per-asset assemble_cards loop in the multi path is also sequential
evidence: for group -> for asset: assemble_cards(lane_i, asset, date_window) runs one asset at a time. obs executor span: p50 1,121ms p90 7,684ms per asset (n=558).
current_cost: 3-asset compare: 3 x 1.1s = 3.3s p50, 3 x 7.7s = 23s p90 of serial fill
proposal: Fan the per-asset fills out via run_parallel (DB-bound on the pooled psycopg2 q(), not vLLM-bound); bound by pool size.
est_saving: -2.2s p50 / -15s p90 on 3-asset compares (N-1 x per-asset exec time)

title: vLLM prefix caching is fully disabled: prefix_cache_queries_total == 0 (not a miss problem — APC is off)
stage: all 1a LLM calls (route/stories/knowledge); biggest win is L2's (cross-lens)
evidence: curl :8200/metrics: vllm:prefix_cache_queries_total = 0.0 — QUERIES are zero, so the cache is never consulted; answers the brief's '0.0% ALWAYS' question (disabled, not varying-prefix). Route re-prefills a ~3.6K-token stable prefix every call (system.md 6,994ch + PAGES catalog; only the trailing PROMPT line varies — measured live, matches obs route avg 3,630 ptok); knowledge 1,426 ptok; l2_emit 22,335 ptok avg (n=1,289). Also found config drift: /v1/models reports max_model_len=65536 while the unit file says --max-model-len 32768 (running server != unit file).
current_cost: ~0.35s route + ~0.14s knowledge re-prefill per run; ~2.1s/card prefill for l2_emit (L2 lens)
proposal: Add --enable-prefix-caching to vllm.service (and reconcile the max-model-len drift before any restart). Gate behind a full determinism cert: pinned-seed near-tie outputs can flip with KV reuse, and the single-asset byte-identical contract must stay green.
est_saving: -0.4-0.5s per run on the 1a serial path; enables the L2-lens win of ~8 cards x ~2s prefill = ~16s/page (cite, not claimed here)

title: Admin '1a' span is max(1a,1b) wall — 1b's 120s tail is misattributed to 1a and 1b reads as 0ms
evidence: stage(run_id,'1a') is stamped AFTER run_parallel joins BOTH layers; admin pairs consecutive record timestamps. Recomputed from 634 raw pipeline_*.jsonl with the same rule: PROMPT->'1a' gap n=2,094 p50=9.6s p90=123.9s max=415.4s (exactly the admin numbers); '1a'->'1b' gap p50=0.0s. True 1a from obs_stage_events: page_selection p50 468ms + story_selection p50 1,540ms = ~2.0s p50 / 3.4s p90. Also: knowledge-gate and natural_compare/resolve_assets time runs BEFORE the PROMPT stamp and build_response t0, so elapsed_ms understates user-perceived latency.
current_cost: no direct ms — but the dominant '1a 120s p90' signal points at the wrong layer
proposal: Derive per-layer rows in admin/latency.py from obs_stage_events (page_selection/story_selection/asset_resolution stamp their own start/end), or stamp the 1a/1b stage lines inside their thunks; stamp a pre-pipeline record covering gate+natural-compare.
est_saving: 0ms direct; prevents mis-aimed optimization of the 120s tail (which is 1b's probes, findings L1A-01/02)

title: Picker re-POST and repeat prompts re-run a fully deterministic 1a (route+stories ~2.0s) from scratch
evidence: temp=0 + pinned llm.seed=42 makes route/stories deterministic for identical prompt+catalog; run_id=sha1(prompt) already addresses the prior result. The injection seam exists (run_pipeline(layer1a=...)) but sets _shared_template which also suppresses reconcile/preflight/reflect — a re-POST needs reconcile active (the asset is now known).
current_cost: ~2.0s p50 (route 472ms + stories 1,536ms) repeated per picker round trip / repeat prompt
proposal: Short-TTL in-process cache {run_id -> layer1a} keyed also by catalog revision; on re-POST inject via a new cached-but-not-locked flag that keeps reconcile/reflect active. Caching a deterministic AI decision — explicitly allowed by the constraints.
est_saving: -2.0s on the picker re-POST join once 1b's pinned path is fast (today hidden behind basket ~6.4s — pairs with the 1b-lens basket cache); -2.2s on repeat prompts (incl. gate)

title: Knowledge gate is a serial prefix on every fresh prompt with a 120s worst-case timeout (no per-stage row)
evidence: _ems_ask completes before build_response for every request without asset ids. obs knowledge_ems: n=316 p50=166ms p90=271ms p99=1.6s max=3.4s. No llm.timeout.knowledge_ems row in app_config -> inherits llm.timeout=120s; a vLLM stall blocks the whole request 120s before the fail-open dashboard fallback fires.
current_cost: 0.17s p50 / 0.27s p90 serial on every fresh prompt; 120s worst-case stall
proposal: (a) Run the gate concurrently with natural_compare + pipeline start; check its verdict before the L2 fan-out (gate p99 1.6s << L2 start ~9s) — decision unchanged, just overlapped. (b) Config row llm.timeout.knowledge_ems=10.
est_saving: -0.17s p50 / -0.3s p90 every fresh dashboard prompt; caps a 120s worst-case serial stall to 10s

title: natural_compare_ids triggers the (cold-183s) asset_candidates probe serially before the pipeline, invisible to elapsed_ms
evidence: asset_candidates() (registry 7ms + tables_with_values cold 183s/warm 11ms) runs to completion before build_response; build_response t0 and the PROMPT stamp both start AFTER it, so the wait never appears in elapsed_ms or the admin table.
current_cost: cold: up to ~183s hidden serial wait; warm: ~11ms
proposal: Overlap natural_compare with the knowledge gate and let 1a's route/stories start concurrently (they never touch the probe); with L1A-02's single-flight, 1b's own probe coalesces instead of re-firing. Stamp the wait in a stage record.
est_saving: ~-2s (the 1a work overlapped) on cold prompts; primarily fixed by L1A-01/02 — this closes the orchestration + observability gap

title: granularity_reconcile re-fires the stories LLM (~1.9s) on the critical path — fired in 43/634 runs (7%)
evidence: run_1a_to uses deterministic route_to (no route LLM — good) but _assemble calls build_stories = a fresh call_qwen(stage='stories') p50 1,536ms. Counted 43 mirror swaps in 634 pipeline files; admin granularity_reconcile avg 1.9s matches one stories call.
current_cost: +1.9s on 7% of runs, fully serial before validate/L2
proposal: Either overlap the mirror stories call with run_validate (validate does not consume analytical_story — verify), or restructure so stories are written AFTER the mirror check (route || 1b-resolve -> mirror decision -> stories once for the final page): 1b's resolve lands at ~0.6-2s, before stories would have finished anyway.
est_saving: -1.9s on reconciled runs (7% of traffic); zero wasted stories call in variant (b)

title: Merge route -> stories into one guided_json call: two sequential LLM hops where decode dominates
evidence: route p50 472ms (3,630 ptok prefill-bound, 32 ctok) then stories p50 1,536ms (342 ptok, 176 ctok decode-bound at ~118 tok/s). The route prompt already lists every page's card titles (measured 7,997ch user msg, 18 pages), so one call can pick page_key+metric+intent+window AND write the chosen page's stories.
current_cost: 1a wall = 2.0s p50 as two round trips
proposal: One guided_json emission for route+stories (same decisions, same model). Only pays off where 1a binds the join (re-POST, repeat prompts, post-probe-fix world); flag-gate against the routing-determinism contract.
est_saving: -0.4-0.5s (one round trip + one 3.6K-tok prefill; total decode tokens unchanged) in 1a-bound scenarios

title: No timeout rows for route; stories at 120s — 1a-lane worst case stacks to ~480s with parse retries
evidence: app_config has llm.timeout.{asset_resolve=60,basket=120,l2_emit=150,stories=120} but no llm.timeout.route or .knowledge_ems -> base 120s. Observed maxima: route 3.9s, stories 8.1s (timeout = 30x observed p99). llm.parse_retry=1 allows 2x120s per call for parse-kind failures (timeout/truncated fail fast); guided_json.route=on makes route parse failures near-impossible so the residual tail is timeout-shaped.
current_cost: worst-case 1a lane ~480s before the fail-closed terminal
proposal: Config rows: llm.timeout.route=30, llm.timeout.stories=30, llm.timeout.knowledge_ems=10 (7x observed p99).
est_saving: 0 at p50; caps 1a-lane worst case 480s -> ~120s (route raise already lands the honest data_unavailable terminal)

title: compare_mode LLM call sits on the multi serial tail after all lanes complete
evidence: compare_mode(prompt) (obs: p50 102ms, n=6) runs after the sequential lane+assemble loops; it depends only on the prompt.
current_cost: ~0.1s serial on every multi response
proposal: Fire it concurrently with the lane fan-out and join at merge time.
est_saving: -0.1s on multi compares

title: Reflect full-page re-route doubles the L2 pass when it fires — rare (6/634) and correctly policy-gated; protect it with the shipped admission knob
evidence: A re-route re-runs 1a (~2s) + validate + a FULL L2 fan-out (5-8 cards / 4-way x 19.8s p50 = +30-40s). Counted 6 reroute_from records / 634 files; preflight_reroute fired 0 times (default policy skips). Timeout-kind hard fails cluster under vLLM contention (documented spiral: N runs x 4 emits each), and llm.global_concurrency (the countermeasure) defaults to 0=disabled.
current_cost: +30-40s on the rare re-routed run; contention-manufactured hard fails are the trigger
proposal: Enable llm.global_concurrency (config-only, sized ~6-8 for the single :8200) so contention timeouts — the main reroute trigger — stop being manufactured. No policy change (honest-terminal semantics preserved).
